<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462289 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00004: A1 Meta-Data Definitions

- page_id: `170462289`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462289/A1+Meta-Data+Definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T07:15:29.174+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint
- labels: ['a1-meta-data-definitions', 'a1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The A1 Meta-Data Definitions view specifies the categories of meta-data tag used throughout the architecture.

Architectures, particularly large ones, usually require meta-data tags to aid with searching and discovery. NAF specifies some built-in meta-data tags, such as:

- Definition.
- Assumption.
- Finding.
- Recommendation.
- Purpose.
- Approver, Approval Milestone, Modeller, Manager, Responsible Owner, Tool Used.

**Implementation**

A1 can be represented using :

- An A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='A1.png']
- An A1 MS Word document report.
- A DARS report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating DARS report in A1' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Architectural Description
- Defines Architecture
- Architecture Metadata

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

NAF specifies some built-in meta-data tags, such as:</p><ul><li>Definition.</li><li>Assumption.</li><li>Finding.</li><li>Recommendation.</li><li>Purpose.</li><li>Approver, Approval Milestone, Modeller, Manager, Responsible Owner, Tool Used.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>A1 can be represented using :</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4cfe41a3-120d-4bdc-90d5-0c3ae656868d">An A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:image ac:alt="A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram" ac:title="A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="A1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="A1 Meta-Data Definitions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>An A1 MS Word document report.</li><li>A DARS report.<br /><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in A1" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Architectural Description</li><li>Defines Architecture</li><li>Architecture Metadata</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462297 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00008: A8 Standards

- page_id: `170462297`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462297/A8+Standards
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-01-08T07:11:34.993+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint
- labels: ['a8-standards', 'a8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A8 Standards table describes the current standards of the architecture and the systems elements that should conform to them. A8 Standards Forecast table provides a description of emerging standards and their potential impact on current systems and services view elements within a set of time frames within the architecture.

**Implementation**

A8 can be represented using:

- An A8 Standards table. [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_standards_table.png']
- An A8 Standards Forecast table. [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_forecast.png']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating A8 Standards table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating A8 Standards Forecast table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Standard
- Protocol
- Forecast

916703221

916703220

##### Description

UPDM: As in DoDAF

DoDAF: A disposition to manifest an Activity. An Activity to be performed to achieve a desired effect under specified [performance] standards and conditions through combinations of ways and means.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462327 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00012: Actual Location

- page_id: `170462327`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462327/Actual+Location
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:29:47.138+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: UPDM Extension

1097442609

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: UPDM Extension

1097442611

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: UPDM Extension

1097442610

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A PhysicalLocation (MODAF::ActualLocation, NAF::ActualLocation) is a location anywhere on the earth. The means of describing the location is a string (locationDescription). The information contained in that string is governed by the taxonomy reference - e.g. if the PhysicalLocation is a “GPS reference”, the string will contain the GPS coordinates.

#### INFO: UPDM Extension

UPDM Extension

This has been extended in UPDM to include non-earth locations.

DoDAF: All subtypes of «IndividualType» Location, such as Facility, Site, etc.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a7666f3-8760-4e5d-97e0-a00244197be0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1097442609</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6e8dec0-2501-492c-8c19-5171978f58f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1097442611</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9bb95e44-8448-4fc8-bfcf-bc80d17fc09e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1097442610</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p><span>MODAF/NAF: A PhysicalLocation (MODAF::ActualLocation, NAF::ActualLocation) is a location anywhere on the earth. The means of describing the location is a string (locationDescription). The information contained in that string is governed by the taxonomy reference - e.g. if the PhysicalLocation is a “GPS reference”, the string will contain the GPS coordinates.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="79b3e519-de8b-4329-a5b3-591a23e9706f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">UPDM Extension</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>This has been extended in UPDM to include non-earth locations.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>DoDAF: All subtypes of «IndividualType» Location, such as Facility, Site, etc.</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>DoDAF, MODAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Instance Specification</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An actual specific organization, an instance of an organization class, for example, “The US Department of Defense”.

DoDAF: [DoDAF::Organization]: A specific real-world assemblage of people and other resources organized for an on-going purpose.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ratifiedStandards : Standard[*] | The standards that were ratified by the ActualOrganization. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart</a></p><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/MODAF.+StV-5+Capability+to+Organization+Deployment+Mapping">MODAF. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462362 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00019: Actual Project

- page_id: `170462362`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462362/Actual+Project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:19:46.621+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Project Viewpoint and Acquisition Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1337141382

1337141384

1337141383

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: (MODAF::Project, NAF::Project): A time-limited endeavour to create a specific set of products or services.

DoDAF: (DoDAF::Project): A temporary endeavor undertaken to create Resources or Desired Effects.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| endDate : ISO8601DateTime[0..1] | An end time of a Project. |
| startDate :ISO8601DateTime[1] | A start time for a Project. |
| part : Project[0..*] | The sub-projects. |
| whole : Project[0..1] | A parent project. |
| ownedMilestones : ActualProjectMilestone[1..*] | The milestones associated with a project. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

## PAGE 00020: Actual Project Milestone

- page_id: `170462363`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462363/Actual+Project+Milestone
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:02:31.773+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Project Viewpoint and Acquisition Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1337194352

1337194354

1337194353

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: (ProjectMilestone): An event in a ActualProject (MODAF::Project, NAF::Project) by which progress is measured.

In the case of an acquisition project, there are two key types of milestones which shall be represented using subtypes - IncrementMilestone (MODAF::CapabilityIncrement, NAF::CapabilityIncrement) and OutOfServiceMilestone (MODAF::OutOfService, NAF::OutOfService)

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| date : ISO8601DateTime[1] | Defines time for a ProjectMilestone. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462492 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00021: Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard

- page_id: `170462492`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462492/Actual+Project+Milestone+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T08:27:55.686+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['actual-project-milestone-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** is used to define a new milestone for the selected project.

To open the **Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard**

1. Open a AcV-2 (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (NAF 4.0), NPV-1 (NAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (DoDAF 2.0).
2. Select a project.
3. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
4. Select one of the available milestone kinds: The Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard opens.
  - Out Of Service Milestone
  - Increment Milestone
  - Deployed Milestone
  - No Longer Used Milestone
  - Actual Project Milestone

The wizard consists of three steps:

1. Milestone name, kind, and date specification. How to specify a name, kind, and date of a Milestone?In this step, you can specify a name of a new Milestone you are creating.You can select one of the Milestone kinds, which are:Out Of Service MilestoneIncrement MilestoneDeployed MilestoneNo Longer Used MilestoneActual Project MilestoneAlso you can set the Milestone date and time. Date and time indicates the position of the milestone in the project time frame. For more information on setting date ant time, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Project milestone type specification. How to specify a milestone type?You can select one (or both) of the given milestone types:DLOD MilestoneDOTMLPF MilestoneYou can create your own milestone type as well.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Project status specification at a particular Milestone. How to specify a project status of a particular Milestone?After creating a new milestone, you can apply the project status according to the selected milestone type. Each milestone type can have specific project threads and status indicators.DLOD Milestone typeDOTMLPF Milestone typeProject threadsEquipmentLogisticsInfrastructureOrganizationDoctrine/ConceptsInformationPersonnelTrainingDoctrineOrganizationTrainingMaterielLeadershipPersonnelFacilitiesStatus indicatorsDLOD AbsentNot RequiredNo Outstanding IssuesManageable IssuesCritical IssuesMateriel Solution AnalysisTechnology DevelopmentEngineering and Manufacturing DevelopmentProduction & DeploymentOperations & SupportYou can also define you own milestone type with a custom set of project threads and status indicators.To create project threads and status indicators1. In the Project Thread area, click the **Add** button.2. Specify the thread name.3. In the Status Indicator area, click the **Add** button.4. Specify the indicator name.5. Click the **Fill Color** button.6. In the **Color** dialog select the color and click **OK**.To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the **Clone** button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators are created.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Milestones in Lr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Milestones in PV-2 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Date and time indicates the position of the milestone in the project time frame. For more information on setting date ant time, see<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying milestone name, kind, and date in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ac:title="Specifying milestone name, kind, and date in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_step1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Project milestone type specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b6f2e06c-d94e-4e52-9132-2028c17d803a" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a milestone type?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select one (or both) of the given milestone types:</p><ul><li>DLOD Milestone</li><li>DOTMLPF Milestone</li></ul><p>You can create your own milestone type as well.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Selecting Actual Project Milestone Type in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ac:title="Selecting Actual Project Milestone Type in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Project status specification at a particular Milestone.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="c969062e-7f9b-45eb-9945-e8af5d9cb358" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a project status of a particular Milestone?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After creating a new milestone, you can apply the project status according to the selected milestone type. Each milestone type can have specific project threads and status indicators.</p><table><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>DLOD Milestone type</th><th>DOTMLPF Milestone type</th></tr><tr><td>Project threads</td><td><ul><li>Equipment</li><li>Logistics</li><li>Infrastructure</li><li>Organization</li><li>Doctrine/Concepts</li><li>Information</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Training</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Doctrine</li><li>Organization</li><li>Training</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Leadership</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Facilities</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Status indicators</td><td><ul><li>DLOD Absent</li><li>Not Required</li><li>No Outstanding Issues</li><li>Manageable Issues</li><li>Critical Issues</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Materiel Solution Analysis</li><li>Technology Development</li><li>Engineering and Manufacturing Development</li><li>Production &amp; Deployment</li><li>Operations &amp; Support</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p>You can also define you own milestone type with a custom set of project threads and status indicators.</p><p>To create project threads and status indicators</p><hr /><p>1. In the Project Thread area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</p><p>2. Specify the thread name.</p><p>3. In the Status Indicator area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</p><p>4. Specify the indicator name.</p><p>5. Click the <strong>Fill Color</strong> button.</p><p>6. In the <strong>Color</strong> dialog select the color and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="80994452-914a-4976-ad03-8607d73c10a0" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the <strong>Clone</strong> button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators are created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying Project status at a particular Milestone in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ac:title="Specifying Project status at a particular Milestone in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_step3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="9679fdea-7166-452c-b060-b6b9497a4189" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Adding Milestones in Lr diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Adding Milestones in PV-2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462147 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00025: Adding Capabilities in Cr diagram

- page_id: `170462147`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462147/Adding+Capabilities+in+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T14:04:59.583+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['add-capability-in-cr', 'add-capability-provision-in-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability .
2. Select the Capability owner in the Select Owner dialog. An empty line for the newly created capability appears on the diagram pane.

To add a new Capability Provision

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Dr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, choose Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

To add an existing Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a new Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Cr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability</strong>.</li><li>Select the Capability owner in the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog.<br />An empty line for the newly created capability appears on the diagram pane.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a new Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Dr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, choose <strong>Add <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">New</span> Capability Provision</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link>.</strong></span></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Cr diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add Existing </strong>&gt;<strong> Capability</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a Capability and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414728880

1414728882

**To add a new Capability**

1. On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability .
2. Select the Capability owner in the Select Owner dialog. An empty line for the newly created capability appears on the diagram pane.

**To add an existing Capability**

1. On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

**To add a new Capability Provision**

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, choose Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462638 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00027: Adding custom columns in tables

- page_id: `170462638`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462638/Adding+custom+columns+in+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-25T11:36:23.301+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Tables
- labels: ['add-custom-columns-in-tables']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add any desired column to your table by clicking**Show Columns** button.

When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.

To add a custom column

1. On the table toolbar, click Show Columns > Select Columns .
2. In the Select Columns dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.
3. Click OK , when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add any desired column to your table by clicking<strong> Show Columns</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To add a custom column</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <strong>Show</strong><strong> Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong> dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.</li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exchange_kind_and_confirms_to_columns_added.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Adding custom columns in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462195 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00028: Adding Milestones in Lr diagram

- page_id: `170462195`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462195/Adding+Milestones+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-20T15:47:43.472+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- labels: ['add-milestone-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a Milestone in Lr diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - Out Of Service Milestone
  - Increment Milestone
  - Deployed Milestone
  - No Longer Used Milestone
  - Actual Project Milestone
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a Milestone in Lr diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462196 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00030: Adding Projects in Lr diagram

- page_id: `170462196`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462196/Adding+Projects+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T11:28:10.492+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- labels: ['add-project-in-lr', 'add-sub-project-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Project . An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Project.

To add an existing Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the Select Project dialog, select a Project (or several Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To add a new Sub Project

1. Select the Project for which you want to create a Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Project .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Project , choose Add new Sub Project .
3. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Specify a time frame for the Sub Project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a new Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Project.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Project</strong> dialog, select a Project (or several Projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a new Sub Project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Project for which you want to create a Sub Project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Project</strong>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Project<strong>, </strong>choose<strong> Add new Sub Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Sub Project.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00032: Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram

- page_id: `170462148`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462148/Adding+Sub+Capabilities+in+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T14:05:11.712+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['add-sub-capability-in-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']or already [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']Sub Capability to Cr diagram.

newTo add a new Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

existingTo add an existing Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

**To add a new Sub Capability**

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

**To add an existing Sub Capability**

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

A UPDM Artifact used to define an alternative name for an element as used by DoDAF, MODAF, or NAF.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| nameOwner : String[*] | The person or organization that uses an alternative name. |

## PAGE 00035: All Views viewpoint

- page_id: `170461677`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461677/All+Views+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:33:48.007+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['all-views-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

566188273

566188275

566188274

“There are some overarching aspects of an Architectural Description that are captured in the AV DoDAF-described Models. The AV DoDAF-described Models provide information pertinent to the entire Architectural Description rather than representing a distinct viewpoint. AV DoDAF described Models provide an overview of the architectural effort including such things as the scope, context, rules, constraints, assumptions, and the derived vocabulary that pertains to the Architectural Description. It captures the intent of the Architectural Description to help ensure its continuity in the face of leadership, organizational, and other changes that can occur over a long development effort.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

566188271

**Related Pages:**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462623 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00036: Applying Actual Measurements

- page_id: `170462623`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462623/Applying+Actual+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-09T13:35:02.563+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['make-element-measurable', 'set-default-value-for-actual-property-set', 'dv', 'apply-actual-measurement']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Actual Measurements can be applied to measurable elements only. 
 
 To make an element measurable

1. Create a Measurement Set with one Measurement at least.
2. Select the element you want to measure and open its Specification window.
3. Add the created Measurement Set as a value for the Measurement Types property.

You can apply the Measurement Set by dragging it from the Model Browser to the element.

As soon as you make the element measurable, define actual values for all measurements contained within the Measurement Set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Define the default values in the Specification window of the Measurement Set and you will be able to use them when creating Actual Property Sets. You can use the default values with just one click of the **Default Value** button in the element's Specification window. 
 
To set the default value for the Actual Property Set

1. Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.
2. In the Actual <measurement type name> property group, click the **dv** button to assign the default value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.
- When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can use the default values with just one click of the <strong>Default Value</strong> button in the element's Specification window. </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> To set the default value for the Actual Property Set</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the Actual &lt;measurement type name&gt; property group, click the <strong>dv</strong> button  to assign the default value.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Applying Actual Measurements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.</li><li>When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462594 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00037: Applying military symbols

- page_id: `170462594`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462594/Applying+military+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-10-20T10:57:15.507+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['applying-military-symbols', 'using-military-symbols', 'mil-std-2525c-symbology']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Military symbols can be applied to resources in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] views.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To apply military symbol for resource shape

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Select Symbol dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Select Symbol**dialog:
  - Create resources from MIL-STD-2525C Symbology toolbar.
  - Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
  - Open the Specification window of any UPDM element, select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] .
2. Select the military symbol you want to apply, specify affiliation and frame style. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click **OK**. 
The military symbol is applied for resource shape.

## PAGE 00038: Applying Project Status in PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `170461726`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461726/Applying+Project+Status+in+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:15:25.536+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Project viewpoint > PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['apply-project-status-in-pv-2', 'edit-project-status-in-pv-2', 'edit-status-in-pv-2', 'apply-status-in-pv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

31012031

31012034

31012032

To apply the Project Status to a new Milestone

1. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Apply Status .
2. Specify the Project status by following the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] .

To edit the Project Status

1. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. Edit the project status following the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']. If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Specify Project Status** and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.

31012030

**Related Pages:**

**true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462197 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00039: Applying Project Status to Lr diagram

- page_id: `170462197`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462197/Applying+Project+Status+to+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-20T15:50:07.442+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- labels: ['apply-project-status-to-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To apply the Project Status to a new Milestone

1. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Apply Status .
2. Specify the Project status by following the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] .

To edit the Project Status

1. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. Edit the project status following the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']. If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Specify Project Status** and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To apply the Project Status to a new Milestone</p><hr /><ol><li>From the Milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Apply Status</strong>.</li><li>Specify the Project status by following the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link>.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To edit the Project Status</p><hr /><ol><li>From the Milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</li><li><p>Edit the project status following the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="09bbfbf5-0d29-4034-afb9-5987f3f65b10" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Specify Project Status</strong> and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

1096137268

1096137270

1096137269

##### Description

UPDM: Represents a visual indication of a connection used in high level operational concept diagrams. The connections are purely visual and cannot be related to any architectural semantics.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462311 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00041: Architectural Description

- page_id: `170462311`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462311/Architectural+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:11:11.677+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1095585534

1095585537

1095585536

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A specification of a system of systems at a technical level which also provides the business context for the system of systems.

DoDAF: Information describing architecture.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| approvalAuthority: ActualOrganizationalResource[*] | References the actual organizational resource that has the authority to approve the architectural description. |
| architect : String[*] | The name of the architect responsible for the ArchitecturalDescription. |
| assumptionAndConstraint : String[*] | Any assumptions, constraints, and limitations contained in the ArchitecturalDescription, including those affecting deployment, communications performance, information assurance environments, etc. |
| creatingOrganization : String[1] | Describes the ActualOrganizationalResource creating the ArchitecturalDescription. |
| dateCompleted : String[0..1] | Date that the Architectural Description was completed. |
| purpose : String[1] | Explains the need for the Architecture, what it will demonstrate, the types of analyses that will be applied to it, who is expected to perform the analyses, what decisions are expected to be made on the basis of each form of analysis, who is expected to make those decisions, and what actions are expected to result. |
| recommendations : String[1] | States the recommendations that have been developed based on the architecture effort. Examples include recommended system implementations, and opportunities for technology insertion. |
| summaryOfFindings : String[1] | Summarizes the findings that have been developed so far. This may be updated several times during the development of the ArchitecturalDescription. |
| toolsUsed : String[1] | Identifies any tools used to develop the ArchitecturalDescription as well as the file names and formats if appropriate. |
| toBe : Boolean[1] | Indicates whether the ArchitecturalDescription is an existing or future one. |
| Viewpoint : String[1] |  |
| Views : View[1..*] |  |
| architectureFramework : Architecture- FrameworkKind [1] | The architectureFramework tag identifies the subset of aliases to use within the context of the ArchitecturalDescription (i. e. DoDAF, MODAF or none) |

##### Extensions

UML Package

##### Related DoDAF views

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='AV-1 Overview and Summary Information' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

##### Related MODAF views

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MODAF. AV-1 Overview and Summary Information' space='UPDM2P190']

##### Related NAF views

[NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NAV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462288 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00043: Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint

- page_id: `170462288`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462288/Architecture+Meta-Data+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T10:13:55.331+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['architecture-meta-data-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint provides information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.

#### PANEL: Views

Views

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462496 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00046: Automatic Instantiation wizard

- page_id: `170462496`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462496/Automatic+Instantiation+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T08:30:59.911+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard is used to automatically instantiate any typical structure of UPDM architecture.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UPDM elements and right-click them.
2. On the shortcut menu, choose Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

The wizard consists of these three steps:

1. Selecting parts. How to select a part?[IMAGE alt='' src='']In this step, select elements and their internal parts to instantiate.
2. Selecting a package. How to select a package?[IMAGE alt='' src='']In this step, select, create or clone the package to store the created actual structure.
3. Creating a diagram. How to create a diagram?[IMAGE alt='' src='']In this step, specify diagram name, select diagram type to visualize the actual structure and the package to store the diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating Structures' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

ac:macro-id="f5b2fc4e-ed95-467e-921d-7520913676bb" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to create a diagram?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461678 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00047: AV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `170461678`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461678/AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T13:08:46.864+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-1-overview-and-summary-information', 'av-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

596919250

596919253

596919251

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between Architectural Descriptions. The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.

The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:

- In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.
- When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.

The AV-1 may be used to:

- Scope the architecture effort.
- Provide context to the architecture effort.
- Define the architecture effort.
- Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.
- Assist search within an architecture repository.

**Implementation**

The AV-1 can be represented:

- Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- Using the following (.docx) reports: - AV-1 Overview & Summary Information. - AV-1 DARS.

The AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.

The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.

596919249

**Related Pages:**

- Architectural Description
- Architectural Reference
- Architecture Metadata
- Generating document based reports
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating DARS report in AV-1' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461682 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00048: AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `170461682`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461682/AV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T13:08:46.980+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-2', 'av-2-integrated-dictionary']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

650486003

650486006

650486005

**Description**

The AV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).

An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.

It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:

- Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.
- Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.
- Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.
- Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.

**Implementation**

AV-2 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.
- An AV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

650486002

**Related Pages:**

- Definition
- Alias
- Same As
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:</p><ul><li>Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.</li><li>Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.</li><li>Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.</li><li>Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>AV-2 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.</li><li>An AV-2 table.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title=" AV-2 table" ac:alt=" AV-2 table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="AV-2_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9af85c59-bc97-46a5-929b-e35704b36d7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650486002</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/Definition">Definition</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/Alias">Alias</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/Same+As">Same As</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462123 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00049: C1 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `170462123`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462123/C1+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:29:36.072+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c1-capability-taxonomy', 'c1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C1 Capability Taxonomy view covers capabilities and the hierarchical relationships between them. It specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures – i.e. one C1 may provide the definitive list of capabilities for a number of logical and resource architectures. In NAF, Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of one or more enterprises. This means that it is feasible to develop a capability taxonomy that will apply to all Enterprise Phases listed in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'], and an C1 may even cover more than one logical / resource architecture.

The capabilities may be organized into specialization hierarchies (taxonomies). A capability taxonomy persists over time (an architect may wish to specify historical, current or future capability) and may be referenced by multiple architectures.

In NAF, a capability is a description of an ability to do something. In C1, the capabilities are only described in the abstract – i.e. C1 does not specify how a capability is to be implemented. A C1 is most commonly structured as a specialization hierarchy of capabilities, with the most general at the root and most specific at the leaves. Measures of effectiveness may be specified for each capability. Note that measures of effectiveness are inherited down a capability taxonomy.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C3 Capability Dependency' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] is the view that describes dependencies between the capabilities shown in the C1 taxonomy.

The C1 view may be used to:

- Identify an existing and required capabilities.
- Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of KURs.
- Provide a reference capabilities for multiple architectures.

**Implementation**

The C1 view can be represented using a C1 Capability Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C1 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Exhibits
- Climate
- Environment
- Environment Property
- Light Condition
- Location

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Measures of effectiveness may be specified for each capability. Note that measures of effectiveness are inherited down a capability taxonomy.</p><p class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">A <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="C3 Capability Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> is the view that describes dependencies between the capabilities shown in the C1 taxonomy.</p><p>The C1 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Identify an existing and required capabilities.</li><li><span>Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of KURs.</span></li><li>Provide a reference capabilities for multiple architectures.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C1 view can be represented using a C1 Capability Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="C1 Capability Taxonomy" ac:title="C1 Capability Taxonomy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="c1_capability_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="C1 Capability Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating C1 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Climate</li><li>Environment</li><li>Environment Property</li><li>Light Condition</li><li>Location</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462126 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00050: C2 Enterprise Vision

- page_id: `170462126`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462126/C2+Enterprise+Vision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:29:51.989+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c2-enterprise-vision', 'c2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C2 Enterprise Vision view provides the high-level scope for the architecture and a strategic context for the capabilities the architecture contains. In essence it describes the end-state for any business transformation activity. The purpose of an C2 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the architecture and to specify the scope for the architecture. The C2 describes the vision, goals, enduring tasks and capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers.

The Enterprise Goals and Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of the enterprise that is being modeled. An Enterprise is essentially time-bounded: it has a start and end date. It is broken into a set of phases for capability planning purposes. The time periods associated with these phases are sometimes referred to as ‘epochs’ by military planners although NAF does not use this specific terminology. It is sometimes necessary in NAF to identify one Enterprise Phase as being that represents the ‘As Is’ capability of the Enterprise. The Enterprise Goals relate to the entire enterprise lifetime, they are not specific to an Enterprise Phase. Each goal may optionally include attributes associated with the measurable outcomes that relate to it.

The C2 only shows the capabilities exhibited by enterprises. It does not show how the enterprise is structured in order to deliver those capabilities – [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'], is used to define the logical structure of the enterprise, and the individual logical nodes in the enterprise that deliver the capability. P1 & D2are used to show the physical and organizational structure of the enterprise.

The C2 view may be used to:

- Capture and communication of the strategic vision related to capability evolution.
- Identify the capabilities required to meet the vision and goals.
- Identify the required timescales for the capabilities (as opposed to Cr which provides a summary of when projects are estimated to deliver capability).
- Identify any enduring tasks the enterprise performs.

It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entitiesstereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».

**Implementation**

The C2 view can be represented using a C2 Enterprise Vision diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C2 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Enterprise Goal
- Enterprise Vision
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part
- Enduring Task
- Capability
- Exhibits

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

An Enterprise is essentially time-bounded: it has a start and end date. It is broken into a set of phases for capability planning purposes. The time periods associated with these phases are sometimes referred to as ‘epochs’ by military planners although NAF does not use this specific terminology. It is sometimes necessary in NAF to identify one Enterprise Phase as being that represents the ‘As Is’ capability of the Enterprise. The Enterprise Goals relate to the entire enterprise lifetime, they are not specific to an Enterprise Phase. Each goal may optionally include attributes associated with the measurable outcomes that relate to it.</p><p>The C2 only shows the capabilities exhibited by enterprises. It does not show how the enterprise is structured in order to deliver those capabilities – <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="L2 Logical Scenario" /></ac:link>, is used to define the logical structure of the enterprise, and the individual logical nodes in the enterprise that deliver the capability. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">P1 &amp; D2 </span>are used to show the physical and organizational structure of the enterprise.</p><p>The C2 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Capture and communication of the strategic vision related to capability evolution.</li><li>Identify the capabilities required to meet the vision and goals.</li><li>Identify the required timescales for the capabilities (as opposed to Cr which provides a summary of when projects are estimated to deliver capability).</li><li>Identify any enduring tasks the enterprise performs.</li></ul><p>It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entities<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> stereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».</span></p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C2 view can be represented using a C2 Enterprise Vision diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="C2 Enterprise Vision" ac:title="C2 Enterprise Vision"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="C2 Enterprise Vision" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating C2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Enterprise Goal</li><li>Enterprise Vision</li><li>Enterprise Phase</li><li>Whole-Life Enterprise</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li><li>Enduring Task</li><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462130 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00051: C3 Capability Dependency

- page_id: `170462130`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462130/C3+Capability+Dependency
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:30:05.858+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c3-capability-dependency', 'c3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C3 Capability Dependency view describes the dependencies between capabilities. It also defines logical composition of capabilities (i.e.capability clusters). The C3 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities and between capability clusters. The composition of capabilities (into clusters) is logical, and the purpose of the clusters is to guide enterprise management.

The C3 view contrasts with [C1](http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/C1+Capability+Taxonomy)which only addresses specialization and composition relationships (i.e. capability taxonomy).

The C3 view may be used to:

- Identify capability dependencies.
- Manage capability (impact analysis for options, disposal etc).

**Implementation**

The C3 view can be represented using a C3 Capability Dependency diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C3 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It also defines logical composition of capabilities (i.e.capability clusters). The C3 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities and between capability clusters. The composition of capabilities (into clusters) is logical, and the purpose of the clusters is to guide enterprise management.</p><p>The C3 view contrasts with <a href="http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/C1+Capability+Taxonomy">C1 </a>which only addresses specialization and composition relationships (i.e. capability taxonomy).</p><p>The C3 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Identify capability dependencies.</li><li>Manage capability (impact analysis for options, disposal etc).</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C3 view can be represented using a C3 Capability Dependency diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt=" C3 Capability Dependency" ac:height="552" ac:title=" C3 Capability Dependency" ac:width="542"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="C3 Capability Dependency" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating C3 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Capability Property</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462133 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00052: C4 Standard Processes

- page_id: `170462133`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462133/C4+Standard+Processes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:37:59.641+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c4-standard-processes', 'c4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C4 Standard Processes view specifies Standard Operational Activities, and optionally traces them to the capabilities they support. Some processes are standard across the whole enterprise, or even more than one enterprise. The Standard Operational Activities can be re-used across multiple logical architectures (e.g. in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']).

The C4 view may be used to:

- Specify a doctrine.
- Trace capabilities to enduring tasks.
- Trace capabilities to standard operational activities.
- Audit a capability.

**Implementation**

The C4 view can be represented using a C4 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Standard Operational Activities will be used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C4 matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Standard Operational Activity
- Maps to Capability

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00053: C5 Effects

- page_id: `170462136`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462136/C5+Effects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:38:16.260+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c5-effects', 'c5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of C5 Effects view is to identify effects, their consequential effects, and the capabilities that can bring about those effects.

**Implementation**

The C5 view can be represented using the C5 Effects diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C5 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Desired Effect
- Desired State

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462139 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00054: C7 Performance Parameters

- page_id: `170462139`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462139/C7+Performance+Parameters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:38:32.881+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c7-performance-parameters', 'c7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C7 Performance Parameters view lists the measures of effectiveness and the Capabilities they are applicable to. The capability requirements (and existing capabilities) are often expressed in terms of measures of effectiveness (MoE). These are high-level metrics used to judge the level of capability.

The C7 view does not present any values for measures of effectiveness, it simply specifies what types of measure apply to each Capability.

The C7 view may be used to:

- Set a Capability Requirements.
- Military Estimates.
- Strategic Reviews.
- Plan an Assumptions.

**Implementation**

The C7 view can be represented using:

- C7 Actual Performance Parameters table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- C7 Performance Parameters table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C7 typical table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C7 Actual table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']Creating C7 Actual table

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Measurement Set
- Measurement

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915142784

915142786

915142785

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A high level specification of the enterprise's ability.

DoDAF: The ability to achieve a desired effect under specified [performance] standards and conditions through combinations of ways and means [activities and resources] to perform a set of activities.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| environmentConditions : Environment[0..*] | The environmental conditions pertinent to this Capability typically including weather, threat, security, terrain, etc. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

## PAGE 00056: Capability Configuration

- page_id: `170462448`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462448/Capability+Configuration
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T13:09:00.824+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

314878458

314878461

314878459

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A composite structure representing the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise. A CapabilityConfiguration is a set of artifacts or an organization configured to provide a capability, and should be guided by [doctrine] which may take the form of Standard or OperationalConstraint stereotypes.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| doctrine : Constraint[1..*] | Represents the doctrinal line of the capability development. |
| deployedMilestone : ConfigurationDeployed[*] | The milestone at which the configuration is deployed. |
| noLongerUsedMilestone : ConfigurationNoLongerUsed[0..1] | The milestone at which the configuration is no longer used. |
| incrementMilestone : CapabilityIncrementMilestone[*] | The milestone for incrementing a CapabilityConfiguration. |
| outOfServiceMilestone : OutOfServiceMilestone[0..1] | The milestone at which the capability is out of service. |

##### Extensions

UML Class
