<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462352 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00057: Capability of Performer

- page_id: `170462352`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462352/Capability+of+Performer
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T13:08:52.248+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Capability Viewpoint and Strategic Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

916399279

916399282

916399281

##### Description

UPDM:A couple that represents the capability that a resource, node or enterprise phase exhibits (Exhibits).

MODAF/NAF: An assertion that a Node is required to have a Capability (Capability for node).

DoDAF: A couple that represents the capability that a performer has.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-1 Systems Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description)

[SvcV-1 Services Context Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/SvcV-1+Services+Context+Description)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38a4a104-879a-4585-a313-121328df7205"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">916399279</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da4f9e34-2cf7-4ea6-80ee-fa8fca55ecd4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">916399282</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87495b6d-8aef-4ce8-a42a-af5a783c4801"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">916399281</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p><span>UPDM:A couple that represents the capability that a resource, node or enterprise phase exhibits (Exhibits).</span></p><p>MODAF/NAF: An assertion that a Node is required to have a Capability (Capability for node).</p><p>DoDAF: A couple that represents the capability that a performer has.</p><h3>Architecture Framework</h3><p>DoDAF, MODAF, NAF</p><h3>Extensions</h3><p>UML Dependency</p><h3>Related DoDAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description">SV-1 Systems Interface Description</a></p><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/SvcV-1+Services+Context+Description">SvcV-1 Services Context Description</a></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462500 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00059: Capability Provision Creation Wizard

- page_id: `170462500`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462500/Capability+Provision+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T09:29:39.336+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['capability-provision-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Capability Provision Creation Wizard** is used to define a Capability Provision by one or more Resources at ta specific period of time.

To open the **Capability Provision Creation Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (DoDAF), StV-3 (MODAF), NCV-3 (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cr Capability Roadmap' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (NAF 4.0)
2. Select a Capability.
3. Do one of the following: The Capability Provision Creation Wizard opens.
  - On the <viewpoint name> diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - Right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .

The wizard consists of three steps:

1. Resources selection. How to select the existing or create new resources?In this step, you can select the existing or create new resources.For more information on selecting elements, see **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**, Step #2.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Capability increment date specification. How to specify a the date and time?In this step, you can set the date and time, when the resource should begin provisioning the capability. The increment milestone is created.For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Out of service date specification (Optional). How to specify out of service date?In this step, you can specify when the Resource should stop provisioning the Capability. The out of service Milestone is created. This step is optional. If you skip this step, the capability exhibition bar on the chart will have the triangle ending.For more information on setting date ant time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- Adding Capabilities to CV-3 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00060: Capability viewpoint

- page_id: `170461684`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461684/Capability+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:28:23.480+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['capability-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

845143062

845143064

845143063

“The Capability Viewpoint and the DoDAF-described Models within the viewpoint are introduced into DoDAF V2.0 to address the concerns of Capability Portfolio Managers. In particular, the Capability Models describe capability taxonomy and capability evolution.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

“The DoD increasingly employs incremental acquisition to help manage the risks of complex procurements. Consequently, there is a need to provide visualizations of the evolving capabilities so that Portfolio Managers can synchronize the introduction of capability increments across a portfolio of projects. The Capability Models included within DoDAF are based on the program and capability information used by Portfolio Managers to capture the increasingly complex relationships between interdependent projects and capabilities.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

800700

800700

845143061

**Related Pages:**

OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart</a></p><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description">SV-1 Systems Interface Description</a></p><h3>Related NAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart">NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart</a></p><p><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/NSV-1+System+Interface+Description">NSV-1 System Interface Description</a><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462122 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00065: Concepts viewpoint

- page_id: `170462122`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462122/Concepts+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T10:10:20.943+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['concepts-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Concepts viewpoint supports the process of analyzing and optimizing the delivery of capabilities in line with NATO‘s strategic intent. It achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision, concepts, NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The Concepts viewpoint further details the dependencies between capabilities, enabling capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.

#### PANEL: Views

Views

1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision, concepts, NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Concepts viewpoint</span> further details the dependencies between capabilities, enabling capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00066: Condition

- page_id: `170462315`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462315/Condition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:27:26.492+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1096512645

1096512647

1096512646

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A definition of the conditions in which something exists or functions. An Environment may be specified in terms of LocationType (e.g. terrain), Climate (e.g. tropical), and LightCondition (e.g. dark, light, dusk, etc.).

DoDAF: An object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Data Type

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A type of ResourceInteraction where one Resource (source) controls another (target). For example, the driver of a tank, one organization having operational control of another, a fire control system controlling a weapons system.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Information Flow

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462590 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00069: Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks

- page_id: `170462590`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462590/Converting+Model+between+Enterprise+Architecture+Frameworks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T11:50:10.765+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['convert-to', 'convert-model-between-enterprise-architecture-frameworks']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To convert an enterprise model to another Enterprise Architecture Framework (EAF)

1. Do either:
  - Change the perspective to another EAF's perspective.
  - Click **File** > **Convert****To** and select an EAF.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Question dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Question** dialog, specify the model conversion options.
3. Click Yes .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462676 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00070: Coverage Analysis

- page_id: `170462676`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462676/Coverage+Analysis
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T14:19:06.575+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['coverage-analysis', 'metrics']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Coverage analysis is based on the calculation of your model parameters. For this purpose, metrics are introduced. You can calculate metrics for the selected elements using the predefined metric suites. Also you can create your own metrics suites from a scratch or based on the predefined metric suites.

Metric is an item of information about some specific aspect of the model. The collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. Calculated at regular intervals they can help tracking the evolution of the model development. The results of metric calculation are stored in the metric tables.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can calculate metrics for the selected elements using the predefined metric suites. Also you can create your own metrics suites from a scratch or based on the predefined metric suites.</p><p>Metric is an item of information about some specific aspect of the model. The collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. Calculated at regular intervals they can help tracking the evolution of the model development. The results of metric calculation are stored in the metric tables.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462144 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00071: Cr Capability Roadmap

- page_id: `170462144`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462144/Cr+Capability+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T07:52:08.427+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['cr-capability-roadmap', 'cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Cr Capability Roadmap view provides a representation of the actual or estimated availability of capabilities over a period of time (derived from capability delivery milestones in acquisition projects). The Cr view support the Capability Audit process and similar processes used across different communities of interest by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The Cr view indicates capability increments, which are derived from delivery milestones within acquisition projects.

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] view specifies the requirement for capability at each Enterprise Phase, whereas the Cr view reflects the output of capability programmes (e.g. acquisition, training, etc.) over time. The requirement specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']may or may not be met by those programmes.**

The Cr view may be used to:

- Plan Capability (capability phasing).
- Plan Capability integration.
- Analyze Capability gap.
- Manage acquisition with High-level dashboard.

**Implementation**

The Cr view can be represented using a Cr Capability Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying all Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Capabilities from Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Sub Capabilities from Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone
- Actual Project

from Cr diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro Milestone</li><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Actual Project</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462301 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00072: Creating A8 Standards Forecast table

- page_id: `170462301`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462301/Creating+A8+Standards+Forecast+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T11:43:22.090+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > A8 Standards
- labels: ['create-a8-standards-forecast-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an A8 Standards Forecast table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.
  - Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Period button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select the Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the **…** button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast. By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an A8 Standards Forecast table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.</li><li>Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the <strong>Time Period</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p>Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f1570457-54e3-4d88-a1ad-2a875582754a" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462300 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00073: Creating A8 Standards table

- page_id: `170462300`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462300/Creating+A8+Standards+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T16:37:24.041+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > A8 Standards
- labels: ['create-a8-standards-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an A8 Standards table, do either

- Add a new element by clicking the Add New button. In the Create element wizard select element and its owner.
- Add the existing elements by clicking the Add Existing button and in the Select UPDM element dialog select the elements.
- Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the **…** button on any cell in the **Standard/Policy** column.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an A8 Standards table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new element by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. In the <strong>Create element</strong> wizard select element and its owner.</li><li>Add the existing elements by clicking the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select UPDM element</strong> dialog select the elements.</li><li><p>Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on any cell in the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462585 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00074: Creating Blank UPDM Project

- page_id: `170462585`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462585/Creating+Blank+UPDM+Project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T11:29:05.044+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['new-project', 'create-blank-updm-project']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create the blank UPDM project. In that project, there will be no predefined structure, so you will be able to make your own. 
 
 To create a blank project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**>**New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctrl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Enterprise Modeling**, select **Blank****UPDM****Project**.
3. Specify name, location, and other preferences if needed.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

By default, DoDAF 2.0 Architectural Framework will be selected in the **General** property group. You can select any of the available Architectural Frameworks.

You can always change the Architectural Framework in the **Project Options** dialog **General project options** group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In that project, there will be no predefined structure, so you will be able to make your own. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To create a blank project</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Do one of the following:<br /></span></span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Select <strong>File</strong>&gt;<strong>New</strong> <strong>Project</strong>. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> On the main toolbar, click the <strong>New</strong> <strong>Project</strong> button.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Press Ctrl+N.</span></li></ul><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">New</strong><strong> Project</strong> dialog, under <strong>Enterprise Modeling</strong>, select <strong>Blank </strong><strong>UPDM</strong><strong> Project</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify name, location, and other preferences if needed.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</span></li></ol><p>By default, DoDAF 2.0 Architectural Framework will be selected in the <strong>General</strong> property group. You can select any of the available Architectural Frameworks.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can always change the Architectural Framework in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog <strong>General project options</strong> group.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:alt="New Project dialog" ac:title="New Project dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_project_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Blank UPDM Project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462125 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00075: Creating C1 diagram

- page_id: `170462125`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462125/Creating+C1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-12T09:49:53.267+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C1 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-c1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C1 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can link Capabilities with the Logical and Physical Resource elements using Exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibition fill in the Exhibits relationship Environment Conditions property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can link Capabilities with the Logical and Physical Resource elements using Exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibition fill in the Exhibits relationship Environment Conditions property.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462208 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00076: Creating C1-S1 matrix

- page_id: `170462208`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462208/Creating+C1-S1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T08:43:51.256+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-c1-s1-matrix', 'build-c1-s1-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows of this matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Capabilities.

To create C1-S1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Service Interfaces).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The rows of this matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Capabilities.</p><p>To create C1-S1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462128 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00077: Creating C2 diagram

- page_id: `170462128`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462128/Creating+C2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T13:51:32.829+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C2 Enterprise Vision
- labels: ['create-c2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C2 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add the Structural and Temporal Parts for the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='create_element_from_smart_manipulator.png'] smart manipulator.
3. Specify the types of parts. The types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements.

Additionally the Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To complete fully the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'], the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C1 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. The Exhibits relationship specifies the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li>Add the Structural and Temporal Parts for the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking <ac:image ac:alt="Create Element" ac:title="Create Element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_from_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating C2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> smart manipulator.</li><li><p>Specify the types of parts.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>Additionally the Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To complete fully the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="C2 Enterprise Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="C1 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. The Exhibits relationship specifies the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

To create a C3 diagram

1. Create or reuse the Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C1 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462135 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00079: Creating C4 matrix

- page_id: `170462135`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462135/Creating+C4+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T14:00:12.895+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C4 Standard Processes
- labels: ['create-c4', 'build-c4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Standard Operational Activities.

To create a C4 matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify the Columns Scope (Standard Operational Activities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Standard Operational Activities.</p><p>To create a C4 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns Scope</strong> (Standard Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462138 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->

- page_id: `170462138`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462138/Creating+C5+diagram
- version_number: 1

- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C5 Effects
- labels: ['create-c5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C5 diagram

1. Create a Logical or Physical Resource elements with Desired States.
2. Connect Capabilities and Desired States using Desired Effect relation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C5 diagram<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Logical or Physical Resource elements with Desired States.</li><li>Connect Capabilities and Desired States using Desired Effect relation.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462143 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00081: Creating C7 Actual table

- page_id: `170462143`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462143/Creating+C7+Actual+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T15:40:06.573+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C7 Performance Parameters
- labels: ['create-c7-actual-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C7 Actual table, do either

- Add a new measurable Capability by clicking the Add New button and select one or more Capabilities that have at least one Measurement Set, defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C7 Performance Parameters' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] ), and then specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells.
- Add the existing Actual Property Sets by clicking the Add Existing button and select the existing Actual Property Sets.
- Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the **Add Missing Measurements** button to update model changes to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C7 Actual table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new measurable Capability by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select one or more Capabilities that have at least one Measurement Set, defined (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="C7 Performance Parameters" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C7]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), and then specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells. </li><li>Add the existing Actual Property Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong> button and select the existing Actual Property Sets.</li><li><p>Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the <strong>Add Missing Measurements</strong> button to update model changes to the table.</p></li></ul><p style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462142 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00082: Creating C7 typical table

- page_id: `170462142`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462142/Creating+C7+typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T14:02:40.041+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C7 Performance Parameters
- labels: ['create-c7-typical-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C7 typical table, do either

- Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Type name, add Measurements, and edit Resources in the table cells.
- Add an existing Measurement Sets by clicking the **Add Existing** button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The new row in the table is created. Type name, add Measurements, and edit Resources in the table cells.</li><li><p>Add an existing Measurement Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462146 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00083: Creating Cr diagram

- page_id: `170462146`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462146/Creating+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-03T12:23:35.280+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['create-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create Cr diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']** opens.

To create a Cr diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the Cr Package and select Create Diagram > Cr Capability Roadmap .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Cr Capability Roadmap** check box.

To create an Cr diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']** is disabled

1. Right-click the Cr Package and select Create Diagram > Cr Capability Roadmap .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter. The blank Cr Capability Roadmap chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>Cr Capability Roadmap</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</strong></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Cr Capability Roadmap</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an Cr diagram manually when <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Cr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Cr Capability Roadmap</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press Enter. <br />The blank Cr Capability Roadmap chart is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select<strong> Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UPDM</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461687 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00084: Creating CV-1 diagram

- page_id: `170461687`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461687/Creating+CV-1+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T13:08:47.141+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-1 Vision
- labels: ['create-cv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355067341

1355067343

1355067342

The CV-1 should be the first product for DoDAF 2.0 architectures.

**To create a CV-1 diagram**

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enterprise Phase' space='UPDM2P190'] .
2. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Vision' space='UPDM2P190'] and define a Vision Statement .
3. Associate the Vision with the Enterprise Phase.
4. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P190'] .
5. Connect the Capability and the Enterprise Phase using the Capability of Performer relationship.

An additional Enterprise Phase can be created and linked to Goals and the Vision by using the goal and vision properties of the Enterprise Phase.

In order to complete the CV-1, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] should be modelled to create the Capabilities that Enterprise Phase exhibits.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75470d55-b66e-4a64-a044-071ad55ac81b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355067341</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="336ed9a4-3300-4f1a-aefa-9f622956cfe2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355067343</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ad3e002-77f7-4351-923f-d27aba4cfd99"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355067342</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The CV-1 should be the first product for DoDAF 2.0 architectures.</p><p><strong>To create a CV-1 diagram</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P190" ri:content-title="Enterprise Phase" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P190" ri:content-title="Vision" /></ac:link> and define a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/Vision+Statement">Vision Statement</a>.</li><li>Associate the Vision with the Enterprise Phase.</li><li>Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P190" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Connect the Capability and the Enterprise Phase using the Capability of Performer relationship.</li></ol><p>An additional Enterprise Phase can be created and linked to Goals and the Vision by using the goal and vision properties of the Enterprise Phase.</p><p>In order to complete the CV-1, a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be modelled to create the Capabilities that Enterprise Phase exhibits.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461690 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00085: Creating CV-2 diagram

- page_id: `170461690`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461690/Creating+CV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:39:27.301+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-cv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414337094

1414337097

1414337096

The CV-2 should be modeled just before the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] is completed.

**To create a CV-2 diagram**

1. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] .
2. Connect the Capabilities using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MD2021x'] (general-specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Performers and Resources using Capability of Performer relationship. Relationship can be associated with environmental Conditions by filling in the *environmentConditions* property and expressing the civil, military or physical conditions required for the realization of target Capability.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f60e19b-dfe9-417d-8196-bee73d1c3818"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414337094</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16baeadf-8566-46dd-b540-af4e734d28ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414337097</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b759e35c-8b2a-4d3d-8610-3a60f9a0ffa8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414337096</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The CV-2 should be modeled just before the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-1 Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is completed.</p><p><strong>To create a CV-2 diagram</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Capability" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Capabilities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021x" ri:content-title="Generalization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generalizations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (general-specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Performers and Resources using Capability of Performer relationship. Relationship can be associated with environmental Conditions by filling in the <em>environmentConditions</em> property and expressing the civil, military or physical conditions required for the realization of target Capability.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461693 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00086: Creating CV-3 diagram

- page_id: `170461693`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461693/Creating+CV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:16:41.874+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['create-cv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414605394

1414605397

1414605396

When you choose to create CV-3 diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']** opens.

To create a CV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the CV-3 Package and select Create Diagram > CV-3 Capability Phasing .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create CV-3** check box.

To create an CV-3 diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']** is disabled

1. Right-click the CV-3 Package and select Create Diagram > CV-3 Capability Phasing .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter.
4. The blank CV-3 chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c68ef6cc-34f7-4772-a0a4-ce8cf1f4096c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414605394</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="793b46b8-cb90-4fbf-9616-36a9d06fc5ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414605397</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ee8eebe-4e83-4630-a7fb-987f25dc9bb9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414605396</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you choose to create CV-3 diagram, <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-3 Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> opens.</p><p>To create a CV-3 diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the CV-3 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-3 Capability Phasing</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press Enter. </li><li>The blank CV-3 chart is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="613f99a8-03b3-4bd8-881f-fad1a6052760"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-3 Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select<strong> Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UPDM</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00087: Creating CV-4 diagram

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415279322

1415279324

1415279323

The CV-4 is created just after the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] is completed.

**To create a CV-4 diagram**

1. Create or reuse Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities with Dependencies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e87fb33-3dd4-4076-bdad-83bb26117340"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415279322</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f97ecdd6-6604-4949-8cc0-4f9b3b2bfdb4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415279324</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c530af70-f0e6-4f58-85b6-dcf633e5698a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415279323</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The CV-4 is created just after the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is completed.</p><p><strong>To create a CV-4 diagram</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Capabilities from <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities with Dependencies.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461705 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00088: Creating CV-5 table

- page_id: `170461705`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461705/Creating+CV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:22:55.718+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?

1415639449

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?

1415639451

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?

1415639450

The content of the CV-5 table are Resources used by Actual Organizational Resources (Organizations and Actual Posts) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the CV-5 table are Actual Organizational Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

A relationship between an Actual Organizational Resource and a Resource is expressed by the Deployed and No Longer Used Milestones. There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the resource is no longer used.

A CV-5 table can be owned by a Capability Phase that will be the context element for this table. The Capability Phase element is related to the CV-1 view.

A relationship between a Capability and a context element is represented by the Capability of Performer.

**To create the CV-5 table**

1. Create an empty CV-5 table for the selected Enterprise Phase. How to create an empty CV-5 table?The owner of the CV-5 table can be only an Enterprise Phase.- Right-click a **CV-5** package as the diagram owner and select **Create Diagram** > **CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping**. An Enterprise Phase is created automatically as the owner of the CV-5 table.- Right-click an Enterprise Phase and select **Create Diagram** > **UPDM CV Diagrams** > **CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping**. An empty table with the column number equal to associated Capabilities in the CV-1 view is created.
2. Add Actual Organizational Resources as table rows. How to add an Actual Organizational Resources in CV-5 table?1. Click the**Add Rows**button.2. In the**Select Actual Organizational Resources** dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.3. Click **OK** when you are done.
3. Add Resources in table cells as table content. How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.2. Click the **+** button that appears at the right of the selected cell.[IMAGE alt='' src='']3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment Milestones wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Deployment Milestones** wizard, select Resources and specify both deployment and no longer used dates for them.4. Click **Finish** when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b656a146-6e29-4bfe-8629-c37f039821e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415639449</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e7f9541-4099-4516-9fef-dfc12fa8ec5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415639451</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26fddf02-da05-44a5-bb77-fa34b562fcde"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415639450</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The content of the CV-5 table are Resources used by Actual Organizational Resources (Organizations and Actual Posts) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the CV-5 table are Actual Organizational Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><p>A relationship between an Actual Organizational Resource and a Resource is expressed by the Deployed and No Longer Used Milestones. There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the resource is no longer used.</p><p>A CV-5 table can be owned by a Capability Phase that will be the context element for this table. The Capability Phase element is related to the CV-1 view.</p><p>A relationship between a Capability and a context element is represented by the Capability of Performer.</p><p><strong>To create the CV-5 table</strong></p><hr /><ol><li><p>Create an empty CV-5 table for the selected Enterprise Phase.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="646d7cb8-f51d-41bd-bf3e-8298743eb9b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to create an empty CV-5 table?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e7da3e5-35f1-4f4e-8f88-8c5ab70f7199"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The owner of the CV-5 table can be only an Enterprise Phase.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>-  Right-click a <strong>CV-5</strong> package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping</strong>. An Enterprise Phase is created automatically as the owner of the CV-5 table.</p><p>-  Right-click an Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>UPDM CV Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping</strong>. An empty table with the column number equal to associated Capabilities in the CV-1 view is created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Add Actual Organizational Resources as table rows.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a23ecdb-5428-463f-a8b5-1eea226eee57"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to add an Actual Organizational Resources in CV-5 table?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>1. Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</p><p>2. In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</p><p>3. Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Add Resources in table cells as table content.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec8885d3-82a8-43d0-b038-2ecc4d47df2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.</p><p>2. Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dr_table_add_resources.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating CV-5 table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><p>3. In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Deployment Milestones wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Deployment Milestones</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select Resources and specify both deployment and no longer used dates for them.</p><p>4. Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461711 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00089: Creating CV-6 matrix

- page_id: `170461711`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461711/Creating+CV-6+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:29:08.439+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-6-matrix', 'relate-operational-activity-to-capability', 'delete-relation-from-cv-6-matrix', 'build-cv-6-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1416217377

1416217380

1416217379

A CV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping matrix describes the mapping between Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that these Capabilities support.

The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Operational Activities.

**To create a CV-6 matrix**

1. Specify the Row Scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Activity Part of Capability relationship.

**To map an Operational Activity to a Capability**

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

**To remove the relation**

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b9a4e95-9372-4053-bfa2-e1046a32c83e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1416217377</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77bc8374-32db-4e62-b4dc-6ebaa9c8e309"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1416217380</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6520e07-0424-4d41-81a6-10dc85407cab"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1416217379</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A CV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping matrix describes the mapping between Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that these Capabilities support.</p><p>The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Operational Activities.</p><p><strong>To create a CV-6 matrix</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Activity Part of Capability relationship.</p><p> </p><p><strong>To map an Operational Activity to a Capability</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p><strong>To remove the relation</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

The rows of this matrix are Service Accesses and the columns are Capabilities.

**To create a CV-7 matrix**

1. Specify Row Scope (Service Accesses).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Service Accesses expose Capabilities using the Capability of Performer relationship.

**To map a Service Access to a Capability**

**To remove the relation**

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462280 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00091: Creating D2 diagram

- page_id: `170462280`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462280/Creating+D2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-08T12:03:46.250+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Deployed Resources viewpoint > D2 Deployed Resources
- labels: ['create-d2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled before [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] view.

To create an D2 diagram

1. Create Organizations and Posts.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.
3. Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.
4. Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Organization Relationship.

You can display:

- the Competencies of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others by using the Commands relationship.
- the Persons and Actual Persons in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] diagram. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled before <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an D2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations and Posts.</li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li><li>Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Organization Relationship.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the Competencies of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others by using the Commands relationship.</li><li>the Persons and Actual Persons in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462597 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00092: Creating Desired Effects

- page_id: `170462597`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462597/Creating+Desired+Effects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:47:07.277+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['select-states', 'create-desired-effect-relationship']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Desired Effect relationship connects the Desirer with the Operational / Resource state. If your Desirer has the state machine (Operational State Description or Resource State Machine), all the states are displayed in the Desirer compartments area.. This allows you to draw the Desired Effect relationship from the Desirer directly to the Operational / Resource state.

To view the states, click the Compartments smart manipulator and select States.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The states are not editable in the element compartment. When you want to edit states, you can do that in the model.
- Double-click the state to open the state diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can choose which states you want to see in the compartment area. 
 
 To select states

1. Right-click the element and select Edit Compartment.
2. Choose State tab in the **Compartment Edit** dialog.
3. Double-click or click the **>** button to select which states you want to see in the element compartment.
4. Click OK when you are done.

If one element has several state machine or region, you will see the names near the states in the element compartment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a desired effect relationship

1. Select a desirer and on smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='desired_effect_button.png'] .
2. Connect it to the desired state.

Additionally, you can create the desired effect relationship in the following ways:

- In the diagram pallet, click the Desired Effects button and connect the desirer to the Operational / Resource state.
- In the Model browser, select the desirer and from its shortcut menu, select New Relation > Outgoing > Desired Effect .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

To create a DIV-1 diagram

1. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exchange Element' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] .
2. Connect Exchange Elements using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MD2021x'] .

You can also use a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] to display Entities taxonomy. Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements.

To relate these UPDM Elements, use Details relationship.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements.</p><p>To relate these UPDM Elements, use Details relationship.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

- page_id: `170461776`

To create a DIV-2 diagram

1. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Entity Item' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exchange Element' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] .
2. Add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Entity Attribute' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] for Entity Items.
3. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association of Information' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] relationships between Entity Items.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Details' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] relationship.

To relate these elements, use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Details" /></ac:link> relationship.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
