- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-resource-types-typical-quality-requirements-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table, do either

- Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. S elect an owning element for the Measurement Set, and then specify the Measurements and Resources to be measured in the table cells.
- Add the existing Measurement Sets by clicking the Add Existing button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> S</ac:inline-comment-marker></span>elect an owning element for the Measurement Set, and then specify the Measurements and Resources to be measured in the table cells.</li><li>Add the existing Measurement Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462240 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00130: Creating P1 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `170462240`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462240/Creating+P1+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T09:55:42.352+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-service-provision-matrix', 'build-p1-service-provision-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a P1 Service Provision matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Resources.

To create a P1 Service Provision matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Service Interfaces).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon is displayed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The rows in a P1 Service Provision matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Resources.</p><p>To create a P1 Service Provision matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns scope</strong> (Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon is displayed.</p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462238 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00131: Creating P1 Systems to Systems Matrix

- page_id: `170462238`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462238/Creating+P1+Systems+to+Systems+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T09:54:22.144+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-systems-to-systems-matrix', 'build-p1-systems-to-systems-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. The P1 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To create a P1 Systems to Systems matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. The P1 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p>To create a P1 Systems to Systems matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462239 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00132: Creating P1 Technology Forecast table

- page_id: `170462239`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462239/Creating+P1+Technology+Forecast+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T12:00:16.228+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-technology-forecast-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P1 Technology Forecast table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Resource as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Resource you want to create, and specify the owner of the selected Resource.
  - Adding an Existing Resource as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Resources.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Periods button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select a Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Click the **…** button on the cell to select the Resources, Standards, Protocols or Competencies to forecast, and then specify the date for the Forecast. By default the date will be the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P1 Technology Forecast table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Resource as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Resource you want to create, and specify the owner of the selected Resource.</li><li>Adding an Existing Resource as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select one or more existing Resources.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Periods button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select a Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell to select the Resources, Standards, Protocols or Competencies to forecast, and then specify the date for the Forecast.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="49fbc8a7-1dd1-4213-8e35-c236a258222a" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">By default the date will be the starting date of the selected time period.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462243 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00133: Creating P2 diagram

- page_id: `170462243`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462243/Creating+P2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-04T08:06:26.140+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P2 Resource Structure
- labels: ['create-p2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P2 diagram

1. Create Resources Roles or drag Resources from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.
2. Specify the types of the Resource Roles.
3. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors or Resource Interfaces.
4. Create Resource Ports.
5. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

A P2 diagram allows to show the provided and required services by the particular Resource Roles.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources Roles or drag Resources <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Specify the types of the Resource Roles.</span><br /></span></li><li>Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors or Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">A P2 diagram allows to show the provided and required services by the particular Resource Roles.</span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462250 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00134: Creating P3 table

- page_id: `170462250`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462250/Creating+P3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-17T13:06:05.456+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P3 Resource Connectivity
- labels: ['create-p3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P3 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P3 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] Resource Interactions to fill in a P3 table.

newTo add new Resource Interaction

1. In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Resource Interaction

1. In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Interaction dialog opens.
2. Select Resource Interactions by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.

- Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] view to create or modify a Resource Interaction.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating P3 table" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating P3 table" /></ac:link> Resource Interactions to fill in a P3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Interaction</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Resource Interactions by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource Interaction.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Interaction Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462255 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00135: Creating P4 Resource Functions diagram

- page_id: `170462255`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462255/Creating+P4+Resource+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-31T09:46:29.423+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-p4-resource-functions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the P4 Resource Functions diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] should be created first.

To create a P4 Resource Functions diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Interactions on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Interactions, do either

- Select Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].****
- In the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the Function Edge.The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462256 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00136: Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `170462256`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462256/Creating+P4+Resource+Functions+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T07:57:17.470+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-p4-resource-functions-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] view.
3. Use Is Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from<span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></li><li>Use Is Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462266 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00137: Creating P7 diagram

- page_id: `170462266`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462266/Creating+P7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T07:54:17.232+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P7 Physical Data Model
- labels: ['create-p7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P7 diagram

1. Create Entity Items or Exchange Elements.
2. Add Entity Attributes for Entity Items.
3. Draw Association of Information relationships between Entity Items or Exchange Elements.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Entity Items or Exchange Elements.</li><li>Add Entity Attributes for Entity Items.</li><li>Draw Association of Information relationships between Entity Items or Exchange Elements.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462269 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00138: Creating P8 table

- page_id: `170462269`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462269/Creating+P8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T08:23:11.768+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P8 Resource Constraints
- labels: ['create-p8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P8 table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the **Add New** button. The new row in the table is created.Select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add the existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select existing Resource Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P8 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> S</ac:inline-comment-marker></span>elect a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</span></li><li>Add the existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong> button and select existing Resource Constraints from the model.</li></ul><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462272 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00139: Creating Pr diagram

- page_id: `170462272`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462272/Creating+Pr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-10-13T16:00:02.458+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > Pr Configuration Management
- labels: ['create-pr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Pr diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in Pr diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button. New Version of Configuration is created.
2. Type its name.

##### Define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select **Resource**.
2. In the **Select Resource** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing resource that has milestones defined.
  - Create new resource (described in the following procedure).

To create new resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the **Select Resource** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**. 
 2. Select package where you want to create a resource. 
 3. Click the **Create** button, choose a resource category**Systems**,**Service**, or**Operational**, and select the desired resource.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 4. In the Specification window of the newly createdresource type its name.

##### Specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

Each resource must have two Actual Project Milestone kinds defined:

- Increment Milestone (defines the start of a period) .
- Out Of Service Milestone (defines the end of a period).

To specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

1. On the left of the resource Specification window, select the **Milestones**property group.
2. Click the **Add** button to define an Actual Project Milestone for the resource. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing Actual Project Milestone .
  - Create new Actual Project Milestone (described in the following procedure).
4. Click **Close** to close the Specification window of anActual ProjectMilestone.

To create new Actual Project Milestoneof the resource

1. In the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create anActual Project Milestone.
3. Click the **Create** button, choose an Actual Project Milestonecategory**Strategic**, or**Acquisition**, and select the desired milestone kind. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdmilestone specify two properties: [ATTACHMENT filename='milestone_specification.png']
  - Name. Type a name of the the milestone.
  - Date . Select the Date property value celland click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Date and Time Settings** dialog choosea date and click **OK**.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462645 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00140: Creating predefined relation map

- page_id: `170462645`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462645/Creating+predefined+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T09:50:12.470+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Traceability and Impact Analysis > Traceability relation maps
- labels: ['create-predefined-relation-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create predefined relation maps for the selected context elements.

To create the predefined relation map for the context element, do either

- Right-click the element, select Create Diagram > UPDM Analysis Diagrams , and choose the relation map you want to create.
- Right-click the element, select Related Elements and then choose the relation map you want to create.

You can also create the predefined relation maps in one of the following ways:

- From the Diagrams menu, select UPDM Analysis Diagrams , choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.
- From the Package shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > UPDM Analysis Diagrams , choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create predefined relation maps for the selected context elements.</p><p>To create the predefined relation map for the context element, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the element, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>UPDM Analysis Diagrams</strong>, and choose the relation map you want to create.</li><li>Right-click the element, select <strong>Related Elements</strong> and then choose the relation map you want to create.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create the predefined relation maps in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>From the Diagrams menu, select <strong>UPDM Analysis Diagrams</strong>, choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.</li><li>From the Package shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>UPDM Analysis Diagrams</strong>, choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461719 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00141: Creating PV-1 diagram

- page_id: `170461719`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461719/Creating+PV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:36:03.460+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Project viewpoint > PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['create-pv-1-diagram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

29486754

29486756

29486755

It is recommended to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] before creating a PV-1.

**To create a PV-1 diagram**

1. Create Projects.
2. Associate Projects by dragging one project (part) to the other (whole). Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.
3. Associate Projects with Organizations ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] ) or Individual Person Roles ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] ) responsible for them using Organizational Project Relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb4f7f06-3bb3-4fe6-a17a-44cc79281125"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29486754</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="152efd29-2bf6-40aa-b4bd-da77bd821ab5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29486756</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9da694b2-2870-4d6a-aa42-fdd77c278668"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29486755</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating a PV-1.</p><p><strong>To create a PV-1 diagram</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Create Projects.</li><li>Associate Projects by dragging one project (part) to the other (whole). Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.</li><li>Associate Projects with Organizations (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) or Individual Person Roles (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) responsible for them using Organizational Project Relationship.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461720 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00142: Creating PV-1 matrix

- page_id: `170461720`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461720/Creating+PV-1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:27:02.503+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Project viewpoint > PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['create-pv-1-matrix', 'build-pv-1-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

29627372

29627374

29627373

It is recommended to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] before creating a PV-1.

The PV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Organizations or Individual Person Roles) for Projects.

The rows in the PV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Projects and the columns represent Actual Organizational resources (Organization or Individual Person Roles).

**To create a PV-1 matrix**

1. Specify the Row Scope (Projects).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Organizations and Individual Person Roles).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca3c2127-9851-4cb3-b9ad-a1b641a4d8b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29627372</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff39839b-02d7-43a4-942e-0347f1e52c68"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29627374</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3aec0792-d756-4eb8-ae1d-c8731e8cc1d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29627373</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating a PV-1.</p><p>The PV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Organizations or Individual Person Roles) for Projects.</p><p>The rows in the PV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Projects and the columns represent Actual Organizational resources (Organization or Individual Person Roles).</p><p><strong>To create a PV-1 matrix</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Projects).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Organizations and Individual Person Roles).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461723 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00143: Creating PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `170461723`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461723/Creating+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:36:33.699+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Project viewpoint > PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['create-pv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

30904153

30904155

30904154

When you choose to create PV-2 diagram, the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a PV-2 diagram

1. Right-click the PV-2 Package and select Create Diagram > PV-2 Project Timelines .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create PV-2 Project Timelines** check box.

To create a PV-2 diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the PV-2 Package and select Create Diagram > PV-2 Project Timelines .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank PV-2 Project Timelines diagram is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c8b1982-ca96-4fa6-9cfb-3727db5a8a47"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">30904153</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5402cee-5787-430b-a82b-f7e72513ecba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">30904155</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a1ef739-a3bd-469b-ae2b-aad25ea4c206"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">30904154</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you choose to create PV-2 diagram, the<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body> <strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p>To create a PV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the PV-2 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c77ed02-f212-4ece-8767-9903e864e6c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create PV-2 Project Timelines</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a PV-2 diagram manually when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the PV-2 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press <strong>Enter</strong>. <br />The blank <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong> diagram is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be5825d5-3d47-4481-9536-da3fe0194dee"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[PV-2 Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UPDM</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> <strong>each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461734 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00144: Creating PV-3 matrix

- page_id: `170461734`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461734/Creating+PV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:34:26.902+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Project viewpoint > PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping
- labels: ['create-pv-3-matrix', 'build-pv-3-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

35757522

35757524

35757523

PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Projects.

To create a PV-3 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify Column Scope (Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Activity is part of Project (“Activity Part of Project” relationship and a part of Capability (“Activity Part of Capability”) relationship). This transitive structure of relations maps Capabilities to Projects.

Project owns Increment Milestones that are related to Resources exhibiting capabilities.

Both transitive structures of relations map Capabilities to Projects. The mapping is displayed in the Matrix.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1c24062-a21b-4cd5-b24a-7d343de04eba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35757522</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fba86877-4b52-42af-809e-65135fec200e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35757524</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aefe6673-d749-46ed-b8a6-333ed3f13f74"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35757523</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.</p><p>The rows of this matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Projects.</p><p>To create a PV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Activity is part of Project (“Activity Part of Project” relationship and a part of Capability (“Activity Part of Capability”) relationship). This transitive structure of relations maps Capabilities to Projects.</p><p>Project owns Increment Milestones that are related to Resources exhibiting capabilities.</p><p>Both transitive structures of relations map Capabilities to Projects. The mapping is displayed in the Matrix.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462234 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00145: Creating Resource Interaction in P1 diagram

- page_id: `170462234`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462234/Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+P1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T14:11:54.090+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-p1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Interaction in P1 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Association and on the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - In the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Resource Interaction Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a Resource Interaction in P1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Association</span> and on the smart manipulator, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /></span></li><li>In the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="0d895a14-702c-4420-8c23-20e1efdc794e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction Manager</span> </strong>dialog</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462244 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00146: Creating Resource Interaction in P2 diagram

- page_id: `170462244`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462244/Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+P2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T14:13:43.698+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P2 Resource Structure
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-p2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Interaction in P2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface or Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Resource Interface or Resource Connector.
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then connect the Resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interactions data.

2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461845 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00152: Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `170461845`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461845/Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T12:07:34.946+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Association and on the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - In the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Resource Interaction Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Association</span> and on the smart manipulator, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /></span></li><li>In the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a2aa563e-be67-452b-ae59-f97ff2723412" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction Manager</span> </strong>dialog</span></span> </ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461849 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00153: Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 Internal diagram

- page_id: `170461849`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461849/Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+SV-2+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T12:03:57.012+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-2-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Interaction in SV-2 Internal diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface or Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Resource Interface or Resource Connector.
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then connect the Resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Resource Interface or Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then click the Resource Interface or Resource Connector.</li><li>On the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then connect the Resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="eefa864d-0fcb-466e-ae3b-8d212a8147f6" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Interaction Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction - labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-4-systems-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Resource Interaction in the SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Function Edge.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interactions data.

pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="135f259e-df6b-4a88-826a-382a79531cef"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Interaction Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461812 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00158: Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description

- page_id: `170461812`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461812/Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+SvcV-4+Service+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T11:17:26.890+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-svcv-4-service-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Resource Interaction in the SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Function Edge.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interactions data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an <span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction</span> in the SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then click the Function Edge.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="01e441ba-f595-4280-8b87-f4201a65392e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <strong><span class="confluence-link"> </span></strong><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction</span> Manager</strong></span></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog that enables to analyze and manage the <span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction</span>s data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462207 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00159: Creating S1 diagram

- page_id: `170462207`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462207/Creating+S1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-15T07:23:31.367+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-s1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create S1 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L1 Node Types' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.

To create an S1 diagram

1. Create Service Interfaces.
2. Connect Service Interfaces using Generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The S1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create S1 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1 view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.</p><p>To create an S1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Interfaces.</li><li>Connect Service Interfaces using Generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The S1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462214 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00160: Creating S4 Service Functions diagram

- page_id: `170462214`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462214/Creating+S4+Service+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-15T07:16:34.819+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint > S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['create-s4-service-functions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an S4 Service Functions diagram

1. Create Service Function Actions or drag the them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an S4 Service Functions diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Function Actions or drag the them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462215 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00161: Creating S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `170462215`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462215/Creating+S4+Service+Functions+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T09:29:13.600+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint > S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['create-s4-service-functions-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Use Compositions or Aggregations (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions or Aggregations (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462224 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00162: Creating S8 table

- page_id: `170462224`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462224/Creating+S8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T09:59:30.947+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint > S8 Service Policy
- labels: ['create-s8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create S8 table, do either

- Add a new Service Policy by clicking the Add New button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL).
- Add an existing Service Policy by clicking the Add Existing button and select a Service Policies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create S8 table<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, do either</span><span> </span></p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Service Policy by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL).</li><li>Add an existing Service Policy by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select a Service Policies.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462690 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00163: Creating Service Channel Summary Matrix

- page_id: `170462690`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462690/Creating+Service+Channel+Summary+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T12:04:35.285+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Service Channels Summary Matrix
- labels: ['create-service-channel-summary-matrix', 'build-service-channel-summary-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Service Channel Summary Matrix describes the relationships among services in a given Architectural Description.

The rows and the columns of this matrix are Service Interfaces.

To create a Service Channel Summary Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Service Interfaces).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Services are connected with Resources using Service Channel connector. Both Service and Resource is type of Service Interfaces that are displayed as rows and columns of the Matrix. Rows are the types of Services and the columns are the types of Requests.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Service Channel Summary Matrix describes the relationships among services in a given Architectural Description.</p><p>The rows and the columns of this matrix are Service Interfaces.</p><p>To create a Service Channel Summary Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Specify the<strong> Column Scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Services are connected with Resources using Service Channel connector. Both Service and Resource is type of Service Interfaces that are displayed as rows and columns of the Matrix. Rows are the types of Services and the columns are the types of Requests.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462687 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00164: Creating Service Channel Summary Table

- page_id: `170462687`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462687/Creating+Service+Channel+Summary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-28T13:44:47.817+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Service Channels Summary Table
- labels: ['create-service-channel-summary-table', 'build-service-channel-summary-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Service Channel Summary Table

1. Right-click the Package, select Create Diagram , point to UPDM Other Diagrams , and then click Service Channel Summary Table .
2. Fill in the table:

- Create a new Service Channel. Click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='New Service Channel wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**New Service Channel** wizard opens. Follow the wizard steps to add a new Service Channel.
- Add an existing Service Channel. Clicking the Add Existing button. The Select Service Channel dialog opens. Only Service Channels are filtered.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a Service Channel Summary Table</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Package, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, point to <strong>UPDM Other Diagrams</strong>, and then click <strong>Service Channel Summary Table</strong>.</li><li>Fill in the table:</li></ol><ul><li>Create a new Service Channel. Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="New Service Channel wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>New Service Channel</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Follow the wizard steps to add a new Service Channel.</li><li>Add an existing Service Channel. Clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The <strong>Select Service Channel</strong> dialog opens. Only Service Channels are filtered.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461902 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00165: Creating StdV-1 table

- page_id: `170461902`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461902/Creating+StdV-1+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T11:42:34.161+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Standards viewpoint > StdV-1 Standards Profile
- labels: ['create-stdv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an StdV-1 table, do either

- Add a new element by clicking the Add New button. In the Create element wizard select element and its owner.
- Add the existing elements by clicking the Add Existing button and in the Select UPDM element dialog select the elements.
- Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the **…** button on any cell in the **Standard/Policy** column.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an StdV-1 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new element by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. In the <strong>Create element</strong> wizard select element and its owner.</li><li>Add the existing elements by clicking the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select UPDM element</strong> dialog select the elements.</li><li><p>Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on any cell in the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461905 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00166: Creating StdV-2 table

- page_id: `170461905`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461905/Creating+StdV-2+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T11:43:51.684+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Standards viewpoint > StdV-2 Standards Forecast
- labels: ['create-stdv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an StdV-2 table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.
  - Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Period button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select the Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the **…** button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast. By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an StdV-2 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.</li><li>Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the <strong>Time Period</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p>Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="782422b5-d615-450d-87e0-01fff970a0c1" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461837 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00167: Creating SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `170461837`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461837/Creating+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T08:48:10.699+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-1 Systems Interface Description
- labels: ['create-sv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SV-1 diagram requires that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']OV-2 diagram should be completed.

To create an SV-1 diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Interaction Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SV-1 diagram requires that the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.</li></ol><p>The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Interaction Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f25cfe69-86d4-405b-a1f1-7da43fb573ff" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461841 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00168: Creating SV-1 Internal diagram

- page_id: `170461841`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461841/Creating+SV-1+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
