### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SV-1 Internal diagram requires that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']OV-2 diagram should be completed.

To create an SV-1 Internal diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Interaction Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SV-1 Internal diagram requires that the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an SV-1 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.</li></ol><p>The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Interaction Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f25cfe69-86d4-405b-a1f1-7da43fb573ff" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461892 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00169: Creating SV-10a table

- page_id: `170461892`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461892/Creating+SV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-11T08:01:25.427+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-10a Systems Rules Model
- labels: ['add-resource-constraint-in-sv-10a', 'create-sv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-10a table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained Systems Element (System, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization Type , Person Type, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction).
- Add existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select Resource Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an SV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Select constrained Systems Element (System,</span></span> Software,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span> Capability Configuration, Organization Type<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, Person Type, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction).</span></li><li>Add existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select Resource Constraints from the model.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461844 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00170: Creating SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `170461844`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461844/Creating+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-10T13:29:43.973+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-sv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to complete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] before creating SV-2.

To create a SV-2 diagram

1. Create or reuse Resources created in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] .
2. Associate Resources using Resource interfaces.
3. Create Resource Ports.
4. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the Resource Internal Communications Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>It is recommended to complete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating SV-2.</p><p>To create a SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Resources created in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</li></ol><p>SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the Resource Internal Communications Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461852 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00172: Creating SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `170461852`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461852/Creating+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T11:03:44.280+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-3-matrix', 'build-sv-3-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To create a SV-3 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p> </p><p>To create a SV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461857 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00173: Creating SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `170461857`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461857/Creating+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-17T13:02:09.570+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-4 Systems Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-sv-4-systems-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SV-4 diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] diagrams should be created first.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].
3. Use Is Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SV-4 diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from<span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li>Use Is Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461858 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00174: Creating SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `170461858`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461858/Creating+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:32:14.176+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-4 Systems Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-sv-4-systems-functionality-flow-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram, the SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram should be created first.

To create a SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Interactions on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Interactions, do either

- Select Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].****
- In the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the Function Edge.The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="4b36e701-10bd-41e6-a4db-1527fbf1fbf7" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram, the SV-4 <span class="confluence-link">Systems Functionality Description diagram</span> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Resource Interactions on every Function Edge.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="d062026e-79bd-468a-8246-2f709fcb55ba" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To display possible Resource Interactions, do either<br /></span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Select Function Edge and on the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">smart manipulator</span> toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">.<strong> </strong></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the Function Edge.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br />The </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> opens.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461863 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00175: Creating SV-5a matrix

- page_id: `170461863`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461863/Creating+SV-5a+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:36:26.782+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-5a-matrix', 'build-sv-5a-matrix', 'relate-function-to-operational-activity-in-sv-5a-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To create a SV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

1. Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p>To create a SV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461869 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00177: Creating SV-6 table

- page_id: `170461869`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461869/Creating+SV-6+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-17T13:11:16.490+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['add-resource-interaction-in-sv-6', 'create-sv-6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-6 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-6 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] Resource Interactions to fill in an SV-6 table.

newTo add new Resource Interaction

1. In the SV-6 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Resource Interaction

1. In the SV-6 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Interaction dialog opens.
2. Select Resource Interactions by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.

- Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] view to create or modify a Resource Interaction.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating SV-6 table" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating SV-6 table" /></ac:link> Resource Interactions to fill in an SV-6 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="56cc6fe1-b993-4f00-b028-14d26f0a3763" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SV-6 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="76c11da5-a57b-4688-8734-3de2a02c662d" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SV-6 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Interaction</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Resource Interactions by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource Interaction.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Interaction Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461873 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00178: Creating SV-7 Actual table

- page_id: `170461873`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461873/Creating+SV-7+Actual+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T12:29:30.762+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-7-actual-table', 'add-measurable-resource-in-sv-7-actual-table', 'add-measures-in-sv-7-actual-table', 'add-missing-measure-types-in-sv-7-actual-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating SV-7 Actual table, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-7 Typical table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] must be created.

To create SV-7 Actual table, do either

- Add a new measurable Resource by clicking the Add New button and select one or more Resources that have at least one Measure Type, defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-7 Typical table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] ). Specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells.
- Add the existing Measures or measurable Resources by clicking the Add Existing button and select the existing Measures or Resources.
- Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the Add Missing Measure Types button to update model changes to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="10b536d7-a3a8-48af-b2c4-26f6a0214908" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating SV-7 Actual table, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating SV-7 Typical table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-7 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must be created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create SV-7 Actual table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new measurable Resource by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select one or more Resources that have at least one Measure Type, defined (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating SV-7 Typical table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-7 Typical]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). Specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells.</li><li>Add the existing Measures or measurable Resources by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select the existing Measures or Resources.</li><li>Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the <strong>Add Missing Measure Types</strong> button to update model changes to the table.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461874 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00179: Creating SV-7 Typical table

- page_id: `170461874`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461874/Creating+SV-7+Typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T13:28:52.695+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-7-typical-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-7 Typical table, do either

- Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. S elect an owning element for the Measurement Set, and then specify the Measurements and Resources to be measured in the table cells.
- Add the existing Measurement Sets by clicking the **Add Existing**button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create SV-7 Typical table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">S</span>elect an owning element for the Measurement Set, and then specify the Measurements and Resources to be measured in the table cells.</li><li><p>Add the existing Measurement Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461878 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00180: Creating SV-8 diagram

- page_id: `170461878`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461878/Creating+SV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-10-14T15:32:07.184+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-8 Systems Evolution Description
- labels: ['create-sv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SV-8 diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button. New Version of Configuration is created.
2. Type its name.

##### Define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select **Resource**.
2. In the **Select Resource** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing resource that has milestones defined.
  - Create new resource (described in the following procedure).

To create new resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the **Select Resource** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**. 
 2. Select package where you want to create a resource. 
 3. Click the **Create** button, choose a resource category**Systems**,**Service**, or**Operational**, and select the desired resource.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 4. In the Specification window of the newly createdresource type its name.

##### Specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

Each resource must have two Actual Project Milestone kinds defined:

- Increment Milestone (defines the start of a period) .
- Out Of Service Milestone (defines the end of a period).

To specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

1. On the left of the resource Specification window, select the **Milestones**property group.
2. Click the **Add** button to define an Actual Project Milestone for the resource. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing Actual Project Milestone .
  - Create new Actual Project Milestone (described in the following procedure).
4. Click **Close** to close the Specification window of anActual ProjectMilestone.

To create new Actual Project Milestoneof the resource

1. In the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create anActual Project Milestone.
3. Click the **Create** button, choose an Actual Project Milestonecategory**Strategic**, or**Acquisition**, and select the desired milestone kind. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdmilestone specify two properties: [ATTACHMENT filename='milestone_specification.png']
  - Name. Type a name of the the milestone.
  - Date . Select the Date property value celland click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Date and Time Settings** dialog choosea date and click **OK**.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461889 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00181: Creating SV-9 table

- page_id: `170461889`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461889/Creating+SV-9+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-11T07:53:42.855+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-sv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-9 table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Resource as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Resource and its owner.
  - Adding an existing Resource as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Resource.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Period button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select the Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the **…** button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Resources, Standards, Protocols, or Competencies to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast. By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an SV-9 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Resource as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Resource and its owner.</li><li>Adding an existing Resource as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select one or more existing Resource.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the <strong>Time Period</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p>Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Resources, Standards, Protocols, or Competencies to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461792 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00182: Creating SvcV-1 diagram

- page_id: `170461792`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461792/Creating+SvcV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:26:42.052+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-1 Services Context Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

984670277

984670279

984670278

You can create an SvcV-1 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] views.

To create a SvcV-1 diagram

1. Create Service Accesses.
2. Associate Service Accesses using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The SvcV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Accesses mapping and Service Accesses interactions with the System Resources. You can model each Service Access internal structure using the SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Internal Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] view as soon as the SvcV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bde705a3-948f-455b-8612-7de7359d064c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">984670277</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fafcc91-61c6-41d4-aa18-2192f2b1d06d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">984670279</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e01dbbe-c69e-4742-8f31-f2d63925cdf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">984670278</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ace814a8-de8a-4410-958f-537737ad7114"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create an SvcV-1 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> views.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a SvcV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Accesses.</li><li>Associate Service Accesses using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.</li></ol><p>The SvcV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Accesses mapping and Service Accesses interactions with the System Resources. You can model each Service Access internal structure using the SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Internal Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a097d0de-1300-4397-955b-7e384401ba57"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SvcV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461783 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00183: Creating SvcV-10a table

- page_id: `170461783`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461783/Creating+SvcV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T10:49:29.143+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-10a Services Rules Model
- labels: ['create-svcv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SvcV-10a table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained Systems Element (System, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization Type , Person Type, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction).
- Add existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select Resource Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="wiki-content"><p>To create an SvcV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Select constrained Systems Element (System,</span></span> Software,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span> Capability Configuration, Organization Type<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, Person Type, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction).</span></li><li>Add existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select Resource Constraints from the model.</li></ul><p> </p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00185: Creating SvcV-3a matrix

- page_id: `170461801`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461801/Creating+SvcV-3a+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:13:46.138+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-3a-matrix', 'build-svcv-3a-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1325539471

1325539474

1325539473

To create a SvcV-3a matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Service Accesses).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461810 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00187: Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `170461810`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461810/Creating+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T08:32:23.033+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-4-services-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SvcV-4 diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] diagrams should be created first.

To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Service Accesses from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].
3. Use Is Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Service Accesses and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SvcV-4 diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Service Accesses from<span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li>Use<span style="color: rgb(0,51,0);"> Is </span>Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Service Accesses and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461811 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00188: Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `170461811`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461811/Creating+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T11:07:43.460+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-4-services-functionality-flow-description', 'display-resource-interactions-in-svcv-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']SvcV-4 Service Functionality Descriptiondiagram should be created first.

To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Interactions on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Interactions, do either

- Select Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].****
- In the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the Function Edge.The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461816 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00189: Creating SvcV-5 matrix

- page_id: `170461816`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461816/Creating+SvcV-5+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T11:12:06.224+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-5 Operational Activity to Services Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-5-matrix', 'build-svcv-5-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-5 Operational Activity to Services Traceability Matrix describes the mapping of services back to operational activities. The rows of this matrix are Service Accesses and the columns are Operational Activities.

To create a SvcV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Service Accesses).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Service Accesses maps to Operational Activities through transitive relationship. It consists of mapping between Operational Activities and Functions using Implements relationship and mapping between Service Accesses and Functions using Activity Performed by Performer relationship.

The SvcV-5 Operational Activity to Services Traceability Matrix is non editable.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>SvcV-5 Operational Activity to Services Traceability Matrix describes the mapping of services back to operational activities. The rows of this matrix are Service Accesses and the columns are Operational Activities.</p><p>To create a SvcV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Service Accesses).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Service Accesses maps to Operational Activities through transitive relationship. It consists of mapping between Operational Activities and Functions using Implements relationship and mapping between Service Accesses and Functions using Activity Performed by Performer relationship.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="d9fc2455-1d5e-42cb-913c-154558a40fe3" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SvcV-5 Operational Activity to Services Traceability Matrix is non editable.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461819 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00190: Creating SvcV-6 table

- page_id: `170461819`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461819/Creating+SvcV-6+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T10:28:26.726+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-6', 'add-resource-interaction-in-svcv-6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-6 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-6 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] Resource Interactions to fill in an SvcV-6 table.

newTo add new Resource Interaction

1. In the SvcV-6 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Resource Interaction

1. In the SvcV-6 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Interaction dialog opens.
2. Select Resource Interactions by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.

- Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] view to create or modify a Resource Interaction.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-6 table" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-6 table" /></ac:link> Resource Interactions to fill in an SvcV-6 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="56cc6fe1-b993-4f00-b028-14d26f0a3763" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SvcV-6 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="76c11da5-a57b-4688-8734-3de2a02c662d" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SvcV-6 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Interaction</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Resource Interactions by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource Interaction.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Interaction Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461823 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00191: Creating SvcV-7 Actual table

- page_id: `170461823`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461823/Creating+SvcV-7+Actual+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T10:30:34.861+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-7-actual-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating a SvcV-7 Actual table, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-7 Typical table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] must be created first.

To create a SvcV-7 Actual table, do one of the following

- Add new measurable Service Access. Click the Add New button and select one or more Service Accesses that have at least one Measure Type defined (see SV-7 Typical). Specify values for each Actual Measurement - directly in the table cells.
- Add existing Actual Property Sets or measurable Service Accesses. Click the Add Existing button and select Existing Actual Property Sets or Service Accesses.
- Add missing Actual Measurements. Click the Add Missing Measure Types button to update table to model changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="575823a1-f081-4701-a563-18009d7ec956" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating a SvcV-7 Actual table, a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-7 Typical table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-7 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a SvcV-7 Actual table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Add new measurable Service Access. Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select one or more Service Accesses that have at least one Measure Type defined (see SV-7 Typical). Specify values for each Actual Measurement - directly in the table cells.</li><li>Add existing Actual Property Sets or measurable Service Accesses. Click the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button and select Existing Actual Property Sets or Service Accesses.</li><li>Add missing Actual Measurements. Click the <strong>Add Missing Measure Types</strong> button to update table to model changes.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461824 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00192: Creating SvcV-7 Typical table

- page_id: `170461824`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461824/Creating+SvcV-7+Typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T10:30:54.627+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-7-typical-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-7 Typical table, do one of the following

- Add new Measure Type. Click the Add New button and select the owning element for Measure Type. Specify Measurements to the Measure Type and Service Accesses to be Measured - directly in the table cells.
- Add existing Measure Types. Click the Add Existing button and select Existing Measurement Types.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select the owning element for Measure Type. Specify Measurements to the Measure Type and Service Accesses to be Measured - directly in the table cells.</li><li>Add existing Measure Types. Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select Existing Measurement Types.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461828 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00193: Creating SvcV-8 diagram

- page_id: `170461828`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461828/Creating+SvcV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T10:37:55.306+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-8 diagram, do either

- Create or select existing Whole Life Configuration element and create its own SvcV-8 diagram.
- Create SvcV-8 diagram under the Package. The Whole Life Configuration as the owner is created automatically.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Whole Life Configuration as the owner is created automatically.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461833 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00194: Creating SvcV-9 table

- page_id: `170461833`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461833/Creating+SvcV-9+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-10T12:40:09.588+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-svcv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-9 table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.
  - Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Period button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select the Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the **…** button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast. By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a SvcV-9 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.</li><li>Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the <strong>Time Period</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p>Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461685 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00195: CV-1 Vision

- page_id: `170461685`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461685/CV-1+Vision
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T13:08:47.065+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-1', 'cv-1-vision']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

845636083

845636085

845636084

**Description**

The CV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of capabilities. The purpose of a CV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architectural Description. It also provides a high-level scope for the

Architectural Description which is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']. CV-1 purpose is identical to MODAF StV-1 product usage. The difference between CV-1 model and StV-1 product lies in different concepts and relationships between them.

**Implementation**

CV-1 can be represented using a CV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

CV-1 provides a high-level scope for the Architectural Description which is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

845636082

**Related Pages:**

- Desired Effect
- Vision
- Vision Statement
- Capability
- Enterprise Phase

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461688 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00196: CV-2 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `170461688`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461688/CV-2+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:07:48.506+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-2', 'cv-2-capability-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1354523297

1354523300

1354523299

**Description**

The CV-2 captures capability taxonomies. The model presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of a timeline – i.e., it can show the required capabilities for current and future capabilities.

The CV-2 specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and user requirements.

The intended usage of the CV-2 includes:

- Identification of capability requirements.
- Capability planning (capability taxonomy).
- Codifying required capability elements.
- Capability audit.
- Capability gap analysis.
- Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of user requirements.
- Providing reference capabilities for architectures.

**Implementation**

CV-2 can be represented using a CV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The CV-2 is used to capture and organize the capability functions – required for the vision set out in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

1354523296

Related Pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability of Performer' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity Part of Capability' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461691 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00197: CV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `170461691`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461691/CV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:37:43.046+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-3', 'cv-3-capability-phasing']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414409397

1414409399

1414409398

**Description**

The CV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e., capability phasing. The CV-3 supports the capability audit processes and similar processes used across the different COIs by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The CV-3 indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).

The intended usage of the CV-3 includes:

- Capability planning (capability phasing).
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability gap analysis.

**Implementation**

CV-3 can be represented using a CV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The CV-3 can be presented as a table consisting of rows representing Capabilities (derived from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] model) and columns representing phases (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] model).

1414409396

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461700 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00198: CV-4 Capability Dependencies

- page_id: `170461700`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461700/CV-4+Capability+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:29:16.384+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-4', 'cv-4-capability-dependencies']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415019815

1415019818

1415019817

**Description**

The CV-4 describes the dependencies between planned capabilities. It also defines logical groupings of capabilities.

The CV-4 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management. In particular, the dependencies and groupings may suggest specific interactions between acquisition projects to achieve the overall capability.

The intended usage of the CV-4 includes:

- Identification of capability dependencies.
- Capability management (impact analysis for options, disposal etc.).

**Implementation**

CV-4 can be represented using a CV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This contrasts with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] model which also deals with relationships between Capabilities; but [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] only addresses specialization-generalization relationship (i.e., capability taxonomy). However Capabilities used in CV-4 are usually created in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] model.

1415019814

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Property' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

It also defines logical groupings of capabilities.</p><p>The CV-4 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management. In particular, the dependencies and groupings may suggest specific interactions between acquisition projects to achieve the overall capability.</p><p>The intended usage of the CV-4 includes:</p><ul><li>Identification of capability dependencies.</li><li>Capability management (impact analysis for options, disposal etc.).</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-4 can be represented using a CV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="CV-4 Capability Dependencies" ac:alt="CV-4 Capability Dependencies"><ri:attachment ri:filename="CV-4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-4 Capability Dependencies" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This contrasts with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ac:link> model which also deals with relationships between Capabilities; but <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> only addresses specialization-generalization relationship (i.e., capability taxonomy). However Capabilities used in CV-4 are usually created in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ac:link> model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07eaaf28-d857-4529-b88d-f0deb5988925"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415019814</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="21d3b4f1-1d38-4fab-acf6-e2a01c6c1f0e" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Capability Property" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461703 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00199: CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping

- page_id: `170461703`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461703/CV-5+Capability+to+Organizational+Development+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-08-16T16:14:45.136+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-5', 'cv-5-capability-to-organizational-development-mapping']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415358073

1415358076

1415358075

**Description**

The CV-5 addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements.

This model shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular Capability Phase. The CV-5 can also be used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.

The intended usage of the CV-5 includes:

- Fielding planning.
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability options analysis.
- Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.
- Identification of deployment level shortfalls.

**Implementation**

CV-5 can be represented using:

- A CV-5 table.
- A CV-5 spreadsheet report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To conduct a comprehensive analysis, several CV- 5 can be created to represent the different Enterprise Phases. Each CV-5 represents different Enterprise Phases defined in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

The CV-5 shows deployment of Capabilities defined in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] to specific organizations.

Deployed or No Longer Used Resources are taken from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

Actual Organizational Resources are taken from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

The timing is based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] indicating delivery of Capabilities to actual organizational resources, and also the point at which those organizational resources cease to use a particular Capability.

1415358072

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying CV-5 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='No Longer Used Milestone' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enterprise Phase' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74876a1b-4e47-406d-b56b-342df239d92a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415358073</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce8de351-be1a-44f3-b8fd-2c608f44ba0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415358076</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cead026-fbed-49cd-acfd-2bd716fd36ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415358075</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The CV-5 addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements.</p><p>This model shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular Capability Phase. The CV-5 can also be used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.</p><p>The intended usage of the CV-5 includes:</p><ul><li>Fielding planning.</li><li>Capability integration planning.</li><li>Capability options analysis.</li><li>Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.</li><li>Identification of deployment level shortfalls.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-5 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A CV-5 table.</li><li>A CV-5 spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="CV-5 table" ac:alt="CV-5 table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="CV-5_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To conduct a comprehensive analysis, several CV- 5 can be created to represent the different Enterprise Phases. Each CV-5 represents different Enterprise Phases defined in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-1 Vision" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The CV-5 shows deployment of Capabilities defined in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ac:link> to specific organizations.</p><p>Deployed or No Longer Used Resources are taken from <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Actual Organizational Resources are taken from <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The timing is based on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="PV-2 Project Timelines" /></ac:link> indicating delivery of Capabilities to actual organizational resources, and also the point at which those organizational resources cease to use a particular Capability.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee8ab54e-bf12-4126-9fd5-c2554ff1271e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415358072</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating CV-5 table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Modifying CV-5 table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Capability Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Capability of Performer" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Capability Of Performer]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Enterprise Phase" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461709 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00200: CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping

- page_id: `170461709`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461709/CV-6+Capability+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:09:24.687+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-6', 'cv-6-matrix', 'cv-6-capability-to-operational-activities-mapping']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415884637

1415884640

1415884639

**Description**

The CV-6 describes the mapping between the capabilities required and the activities that enable those capabilities.

It is important to ensure that the operational activity matches the required capability. The CV-6 DoDAF-described Model provides a bridge between capability analyzed using CVs and operational activities analyzed using OVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix. The capability to activity mappings may include both situations where activities fully satisfy the desired capability and those where the activity only partially meets the capability requirement.

The intended usage of the CV-6 includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to operational activities.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

CV-6 can be represented using a CV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities are used as row elements and the Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] – but provides the interface between Capability and Operational Models rather than Operational to System Models.

1415884636

**Related Page:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity Part of Capability' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
