The CV-6 DoDAF-described Model provides a bridge between capability analyzed using CVs and operational activities analyzed using OVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix. The capability to activity mappings may include both situations where activities fully satisfy the desired capability and those where the activity only partially meets the capability requirement.</p><p>The intended usage of the CV-6 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing capability requirements to operational activities.</li><li>Capability audit.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-6 can be represented using a CV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities are used as row elements and the Operational Activities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping" ac:alt="CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="CV-6.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This model is analogous to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> – but provides the interface between Capability and Operational Models rather than Operational to System Models.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d773adc2-77f0-48e6-818e-2e24c7776074"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415884636</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2aa70e64-ec49-49fb-843d-7d0b9b529a0a" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Activity Part of Capability" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity Part Of Capability]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461712 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00201: CV-7 Capability to Services Mapping

- page_id: `170461712`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461712/CV-7+Capability+to+Services+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:13:23.842+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-7', 'cv-7-capability-to-services-mapping', 'cv-7-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1416278289

1416278291

1416278290

**Description**

The CV-7 describes the mapping between the capabilities required and the services that enable those capabilities.

It is important to ensure that the services match the required capability. The CV-7 provides a bridge between capability analyzed using CVs and services analyzed using SvcVs. Specifically, it identifies how services can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5a which maps system functions to operational activities. The capability to service mappings may include both situations where a service fully satisfies the desired capability and those where the service only partially meets the capability requirement.

The intended usage of the CV-7 includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to services.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

CV-7 can be represented using a CV-7 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. Service Interfaces are used as row elements and Capabilities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix – but provides the interface between Capability and Service Models rather than Operational to System Models.

1416278288

Related Pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Access' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability of Performer' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity Part of Capability' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

The capability to service mappings may include both situations where a service fully satisfies the desired capability and those where the service only partially meets the capability requirement.</p><p>The intended usage of the CV-7 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing capability requirements to services.</li><li>Capability audit.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-7 can be represented using a CV-7 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462276 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00202: D1 Master Data

- page_id: `170462276`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462276/D1+Master+Data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:39:35.576+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Deployed Resources viewpoint
- labels: ['d1-master-data', 'd1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The D1 Master Data view specifies standard reference data in the deployed space. This includes organizations, locations, facilities, and individual platforms. The D1 view presents all the data as a hierarchy and provides a text definition for each one by displaying the following properties:

- The name used for this element in the architecture.
- Alternative names for this element, for example, if the element is listed in the NATO ontology it may have multiple names.
- A brief description of the element.

**Implementation** 
The D1 view can be represented using:

- An D1 table. [ATTACHMENT filename='D1_table.png']

- A D1 MS Word document report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Definition
- Alias
- Same As
- UPDM Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The D1 view presents all the data as a hierarchy and provides a text definition for each one by displaying the following properties:</p><ul><li>The name used for this element in the architecture.</li><li>Alternative names for this element, for example, if the element is listed in the NATO ontology it may have multiple names.</li><li>A brief description of the element.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong><br />The D1 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>An D1 table.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="D1 Master Data table" ac:title="D1 Master Data table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="D1_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="D1 Master Data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1e59897a-7dce-4be7-8cb2-5684f1021479">A D1 MS Word document report.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Definition</li><li>Alias</li><li>Same As</li><li>UPDM Element</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462278 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00203: D2 Deployed Resources

- page_id: `170462278`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462278/D2+Deployed+Resources
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:39:49.082+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Deployed Resources viewpoint
- labels: ['d2-deployed-resources', 'd2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The D2 Deployed Resources view covers the structure of specific, individual Resources. This could be actual instances of systems, organizations or natural resources.

The D2 shows how specific individual resources are deployed and configured. The scope for the D2 view is broad and covers human and non-human resources. This view can therefore be used for depicting organizational structures.

The D2 view may be used to:

- Specify configuration of deployed resources.
- Identify architecture stakeholders.
- Identify process owners.
- Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

The D2 view can be represented using:

- An D2 Deployed Resources diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='D2.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating D2 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating Structures' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Organization
- Post
- Person
- Command
- Competence
- Provides Competence
- Requires Competence
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Actual Person
- Fills Post
- Actual Organization Relationship
- Actual Organization Role
- Owns Process

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00204: Data and Information viewpoint

- page_id: `170461770`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:10:45.987+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['data-and-information-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

638453214

638453216

638453215

“DoDAF-described Models within the Data and Information Viewpoint provide a means of portraying the operational and business information requirements and rules that are managed within and used as constraints on the organizations business activities. Experience gained from many enterprise architecture efforts within the DoD led to the identification of several levels of abstraction necessary to accurately communicate the information needs of an organization or enterprise. The appropriate level or levels of abstraction for a given architecture are dependent on the use and the intended users of the architecture. Where appropriate, the data captured in this viewpoint needs to be considered by COIs.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

**Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration**

You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for conceptual, logical, and physical data modeling in all views of the Data and Information Viewpoint. It supports the information engineering notation within these views.

638453212

**Related Pages:**

Experience gained from many enterprise architecture efforts within the DoD led to the identification of several levels of abstraction necessary to accurately communicate the information needs of an organization or enterprise. The appropriate level or levels of abstraction for a given architecture are dependent on the use and the intended users of the architecture. Where appropriate, the data captured in this viewpoint needs to be considered by COIs.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p><p><strong>Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration</strong></p><p>You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for conceptual, logical, and physical data modeling in all views of the Data and Information Viewpoint. It supports the information engineering notation within these views.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92f8d61c-1ff4-4508-b657-bc841db9feb5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">638453212</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="0eab76c7-9323-48a9-8aae-cc175fee1043" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462504 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00205: Date and Time Settings dialog

- page_id: `170462504`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462504/Date+and+Time+Settings+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T09:37:46.666+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['date-and-time-settings-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Date and Time Settings** dialog is provided for defining a particular date and time. The dialog supports ISO8601, the International Standard for the representation of date and time.

The dialog can be usedfor elements or views that have date and time properties (e.g. Actual Project Milestone, Actual Project Enterprise Phase, etc.).

To open the **Date and Time Settings** dialog

1. Open element's Specification window.
2. Select the Date property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='[...]button_small.png'] . The Date and Time Settings dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Dialog element | Element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Set the date and time | Drop-down list | A date and time value that exists in the model.Select a new date and time value from the drop-down list. |
| Select timeline | Text box | A package for the data grouping. |
| Date | Visual calendar | A date value in the visual calendar.To select a day, click the day on the calendar.To move through months, use arrows on the left and on the right in the title section of the calendar.To move through years, use double-arrows on the left and on the right in the title section of the calendar. |
| Date | Spin box | A date value in the text format (“YYYY-MM-DD”).To change the time value you can do one of the followingType a new valid date value.Click up and down arrows to increase or decrease the current value. |
| Time | Spin box | A time value in the text format (“hh-ss-mm”).To change the time value you can do one of the followingType a new valid time value.Click up and down arrows to increase or decrease the current value. |
| Time zone | Drop-down list | A time zone value.Select a new time zone value from the drop-down list.The default time zone value is the one of your computer regional settings. |

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [...] (by the Select timeline box) | Click to select or create a new Timeline. The Select Timeline dialog opens. |
| Today | Click to set the Date box value to the current date of your computer. |
| Now | Click to set the Time box value to the current time of your computer. |
| Default | Click to set the Time zone box value to the value that is set in your computer. |

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Capability Provision Creation Wizard
- Deployment Milestones wizard

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00206: Defines Architecture

- page_id: `170462319`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`

- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097099203

1097099205

1097099204

##### Description

UPDM: An ArchitecturalDescription describes the architecture for an EnterprisePhase. The DefinesArchitecture stereotype establishes a relationship between ArchitecturalDescription and EnterprisePhase.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Realization

##### Related DoDAF views

[AV-1 Overview and Summary Information](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information)

##### Related MODAF views

[AV-1 Overview and Summary Information](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information)

##### Related NAF views

[NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/NAV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information)

## PAGE 00208: Deleting Capabilities from CV-3 diagram

- page_id: `170461698`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`

- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['delete-capability-from-cv-3', 'delete-from-chart-in-cv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414795219

1414795221

1414795220

**To delete a Capability from the chart**

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

**To delete a Capability from the model**

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8510197-5469-48dc-a0c2-3ca2cb9418be"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414795219</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ca8071f-18f1-477b-996c-5cca7176b87a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414795221</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b71fee7e-f4b6-4c93-b89a-0d7dfe63b1c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414795220</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><br /></p><p><strong>To delete a Capability from the chart</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong> <strong>Delete From Chart </strong>button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong>To delete a Capability from the model</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong> <strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To delete a Milestone

1. Select a Milestone.
2. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Delete .

## PAGE 00213: Deleting Sub Capabilities from CV-3 diagram

- page_id: `170461699`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461699/Deleting+Sub+Capabilities+from+CV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:37:52.740+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['delete-from-chart-in-cv-3', 'delete-sub-capability-from-cv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414814072

1414814075

1414814074

**To delete a Sub Capability from the chart**

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

**To delete a Sub Capability from the model**

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

915397083

915397086

915397084

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that an ActualOrganisationResource started to use, or is slated to start using a CapabilityConfiguration from a specific point in time.This is used to describe capabilities going into service with specific organizations or posts.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| configuration : CapabilityConfiguration[1..*] | Affected CapabilityConfigurations. |
| usedBy : ActualOrganizationalResource[1..*] | ActualOrganizationalResources using CapabilityConfiguration deployed at this Milestone. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462506 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00216: Deployment Milestones wizard

- page_id: `170462506`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462506/Deployment+Milestones+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T09:44:55.968+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['deployment-milestones-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the **Deployment Milestones** wizard is to define Capability deployment in a particular time frame of the enterprise life cycle.

To open the **Deployment Milestones** wizard do the following

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (DoDAF), StV-5 (MODAF), NCV-5 (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dr Deployment Schedule' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (NAF 4.0) table.
2. Click a cell wherein you want to add a Capability Deployment.
3. Click the + button that appears on the right side of the cell. [ATTACHMENT filename='Dr_table_add_resources.png']

The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the Actual Organizational Resource responsible for that Capability deployment.

The wizard consists of three steps:

1. Select Resources. How to select Resources?In this step you are defining Resources required for the deployment of Capability. You can search for existing Resources or create a new one.[IMAGE alt='' src='']You can finish the Deployment Milestones creation after the wizard’s first step, if you do not need to set other than default start and end dates.
2. Set the start date and time for the selected Resources to be used by the Actual Organizational Resource in deploying the Capability. How to specify the start date and time for the selected Resources?In this step you can specify the start date and time for the Capability Deployment with selected Resources. For the date and time specification the wizard provides you with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Date and Time Setting** dialog. By default selected start date and time are the same as corresponding dates of the table context (Enterprise Phase).The start date for the Resource usage can not be later than the end date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Deployment Milestones** wizard only if start date is specified.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Set the end date and time for the selected Resources to be no longer used by the Actual Organizational Resource in deploying the Capability. How to specify the end date and time for the selected Resources?In this step you can specify the end date and time for the Capability Deployment with selected Resources. For the date and time specification the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. By default selected end date and time are the same as corresponding dates of the table context (Enterprise Phase).The end date for the Resource usage can not be earlier than the start date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Deployment Milestones** wizard if the end date is not specified.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Elements that can be Actual Organizational Resources are listed in the following table.

|  | DoDAF | MODAF / NAF/ NAF 4.0 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Actual Organizational Resources | OrganizationIndividual PersonRole | Actual OrganizationActual Post |

An Actual Organizational Resource uses assigned Resources to deploy a Capability in a specified time period. On the basis of the data specified in the wizard the following actions are taken:

1. Resources are linked to the Actual Organizational Resource through a pair of Deployment and No Longer Used Milestones.
2. Start and end dates are set for a Resource usage through a pair of Deployment and No Longer Used Milestones.
3. Capability of Performer (DoDAF) or an Exhibits (MODAF/NAF) relationship is created between Resources and the Capability. The relationships are not duplicated. If a relationship already exists, it is reused.

The wizard suggests you default start and end dates for the Resource usage. The dates matches start and end dates of an Enterprise Phase. As soon as the dates are defined Yukon finish the wizard while being in the first.

The Resource and its Deployed and No Longer Used Milestones are created in the same package that already contains the Enterprise Phase (the time frame of the Capability deployment).

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Dr table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']Creating Dr table
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying CV-5 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

By default selected start date and time are the same as corresponding dates of the table context (Enterprise Phase).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="2aeaf087-6e32-4bfb-8357-ed8801f7246a" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The start date for the Resource usage can not be later than the end date of the table context. You will be able to finish the <strong>Deployment Milestones</strong> wizard only if start date is specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying deployment date in Deployment Milestones wizard" ac:title="Specifying deployment date in Deployment Milestones wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_milestones_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Deployment Milestones wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Set the end date and time for the selected Resources to be no longer used by the Actual Organizational Resource in deploying the Capability.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="011aa112-64ad-43e3-a5aa-b2d4ce9e1adb" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify the end date and time for the selected Resources?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this step you can specify the end date and time for the Capability Deployment with selected Resources. For the date and time specification the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. By default selected end date and time are the same as corresponding dates of the table context (Enterprise Phase).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="22148d28-e480-4577-83a3-a50b5eb00146" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The end date for the Resource usage can not be earlier than the start date of the table context. You will be able to finish the <strong>Deployment Milestones</strong> wizard if the end date is not specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying no longer used date in Deployment Milestones wizard" ac:title="Specifying no longer used date in Deployment Milestones wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_milestones_wizard_step3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Deployment Milestones wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>Elements that can be Actual Organizational Resources are listed in the following table.</p><table><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>DoDAF</th><th><p>MODAF / NAF/ NAF 4.0</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Actual Organizational Resources</strong></p></td><td><p>Organization</p><p>Individual Person</p><p>Role</p></td><td><p>Actual Organization</p><p>Actual Post</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>An Actual Organizational Resource uses assigned Resources to deploy a Capability in a specified time period. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462511 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00219: Diagram Creation Wizard

- page_id: `170462511`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462511/Diagram+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T09:50:01.805+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['diagram-creation-wizard', 'cv-3-creation-wizard', 'pv-2-creation-wizard', 'acv-2-creation-wizard', 'stv-3-creation-wizard', 'npv-1-creation-wizard', 'ncv-5-creation-wizard', 'lr-lines-of-development-creation-wizard', 'cr-capability-roadmap-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Diagram Creation Wizard** is used to create the following diagrams:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (DoDAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (DoDAF)
- AcV-2 Programme Timelines (MODAF)
- StV-3 Capability Phasing (MODAF)
- NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships (NAF v.3)
- NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping (NAF v.3)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lr Lines of Development' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (NAF v.4)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cr Capability Roadmap' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (NAF v.4)

The name of this wizard depends on particular diagram name: **<diagram name> Creation Wizard**:

- CV-3 Creation Wizard
- PV-2 Creation Wizard
- AcV-2 Creation Wizard
- StV-3 Creation Wizard
- NPV-1 Creation Wizard
- NCV-5 Creation Wizard
- Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard
- Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard

To open the **Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> . The Diagram Creation Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

1. Name and owning package specification. How to specify a name and owning package?In this step, you can specify the diagram name and select or create the owning package. If you want to create a new owning package, use the **Create Owner** button. Also, you can clone an existing package by clicking the **Clone** button.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Capability selection. How to select Capabilities?In this step, you can select Capabilities from the list.[IMAGE alt='' src='']ButtonDescription[IMAGE alt='' src='']Selects a Capability.Alternatively, double-click the Capability.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Removes a Capability.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Removes all Capabilities.
3. The chart property setting. How to set the chart properties?In this step, you can set the display style of the chart.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you do not wish to use the this wizard next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create <diagram name>** check box.

To restore the usage of the**Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- Creating Cr diagram
- Creating CV-3 diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating PV-2 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00221: Displaying all Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram

- page_id: `170462149`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462149/Displaying+all+Sub+Capabilities+in+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-04T11:10:11.658+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['display-all-in-cr', 'display-sub-capability-in-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display all Sub Capabilities

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.
2. In the Cr diagram toolbar, click the Display All button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To display all Sub Capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.</li><li>In the Cr diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Display All</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462628 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00223: Displaying Custom Measurements

- page_id: `170462628`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462628/Displaying+Custom+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T11:04:36.271+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['add-measurement', 'delete-measurement', 'remove-measurement', 'clear-all', 'display-custom-measurements']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display custom Measurements in addition to predefined ones in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (NOV-3), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (NSV-6), and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] tables by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Custom Measurements' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']. Measurements can be displayed for the Resource Interactions, Operational Exchanges (in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (NOV-3) table), Operational Exchange, and Resource Interaction. 
When you are adding a new Measurement column, Measurements are collected from the elements used only in the active table. The collected Measurements are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurements dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Measurements** dialog. You can select the desired Measurement which will be displayed as a newly created column in the table.

You can manage custom Measurements byremoving them[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Custom Measurements' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']. 
 
addTo add a column with Measurements

1. On the table toolbar, click **Show** **Columns** > **Measurements**.
2. In the******Measurements**dialog, click the **Operational** **Exchange** (or **Resource** **Interaction**) or **Operational** **Exchange** **Item** **([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'],** **NOV-3)** (or **Resource** **Interaction** **Item** **([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'],** **NSV-6)**) tab.
3. In the selected tab, click to choose the desired Measurement.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

removeTo remove columns with Measurements

1. On the table toolbar, click **Show** **Columns** > **Measurements**.
2. In the **Measurements**dialog, click the **Operational** **Exchange** (or **Resource** **Interaction**) or **Operational** **Exchange** **Item** **([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'],** **NOV-3)** (or **Resource** **Interaction** **Item** **([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'],** **NSV-6)**) tab.
3. In the opened tab, click the **Clear** **All** button to clear all the selected check boxes.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

The Measurement column is automatically removed after doing one of the following action:

- Removing the Operational Exchange from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed Operational Exchange is used by the other Operational Exchange in the table.
- Removing the conveyed element from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed conveyed element is used by the other Operational Exchange Item ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] , NOV-3) (or Resource Interaction Item ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] , NSV-6)) in the table.
- Removing the Measurement Set which includes the Measurement represented by a table column from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>Item</strong> <strong>(<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</strong> <strong>NSV-6)</strong>) tab. </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the selected tab, click to choose the desired Measurement.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done. </span></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="08505d56-d8c8-4f19-bdb6-7304ea23017d" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To remove columns with Measurements</span></p><hr /><ol><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">On the table <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">toolbar</span></span>, click <strong>Show</strong> <strong>Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Measurements</strong>.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong>Measurements</strong></span> </span>dialog</span>, click the <strong>Operational</strong> <strong>Exchange</strong> (or <strong>Resource</strong> <strong>Interaction</strong>) or <strong>Operational</strong> <strong>Exchange</strong> <strong>Item</strong> <strong>(<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</strong> <strong>NOV-3)</strong> (or <strong>Resource</strong> <strong>Interaction</strong> <strong>Item</strong> <strong>(<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</strong> <strong>NSV-6)</strong>) tab.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the opened tab, click the <strong>Clear</strong> <strong>All</strong> button to clear all the selected check boxes.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="46e39ee0-6028-478a-94fe-36a95b282b78" ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Measurement column is automatically removed after doing one of the following action:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Removing the Operational Exchange from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed Operational Exchange is used by the other Operational Exchange in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Removing the conveyed element from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed conveyed element is used by the other Operational Exchange Item (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, NOV-3) (or Resource Interaction Item (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, NSV-6)) in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Removing the Measurement Set which includes the Measurement represented by a table column from the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462649 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00224: Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties

- page_id: `170462649`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462649/Displaying+information+indirectly+related+to+element+through+derived+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-28T11:48:16.935+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Traceability and Impact Analysis
- labels: ['create-derived-properties', 'edit-derived-properties', 'delete-derived-properties', 'display-derived-properties', 'display-information-indirectly-related-to-element-through-derived-properties']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

While working with your models, sometimes in the same diagram or table, you need to see information that is not directly related to an element. You can display that information in the following ways:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] (as a new column)

To display the information indirectly related to an element, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']. A property whose values are calculated is called a derived property.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] derived properties in the:

- Element’s Specification window
- Table

You can manage derived properties using the **Expression** dialog.

create_newTo create a new derived property

1. Open the Expression dialog by doing one of the following:
  - In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group and then click the Create button.
  - In the toolbar of an active table, click Show Columns and then select New Derived Property .
2. In the Expression dialog, specify the name and expression for the derived property.
3. Click **OK**.

If you want to create a derived property with the result other than an element, you need to change the derived property type manually.

editTo edit a derived property

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and then click the Edit button.
  - In the active table, from the derived property column heading’s shortcut menu, select Edit Expression.
2. In the Expression dialog make changes and click OK .

removeTo remove a derived property

1. Do one of the following:

- In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and then click the Delete button.
- In the active table, from the derived property column heading’s shortcut menu, select Remove Derived Property .

display_on_noteTo display derived properties on a Note shape

1. Create a Note for the selected element or select an existing one.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select a Note and click the Edit Element Properties smart manipulator.The Compartment Edit dialog opens.
  - Right-click the Note shape and select Edit Compartment . The Compartment Edit dialog opens. Choose Element Properties tab.
3. In the**Hidden** list, move properties you want to display on a Note shape to the **Selected** list by doing one of the following:
  - Select one or more properties and click the > or >> button.
  - Double-click to select properties one by one.
4. Click OK . Selected derived properties are displayed in the Note.

display_in_tableTo display derived properties in a table

1. On the table toolbar, click Show Columns button and select Select Columns .
2. In the opened Select Columns dialog, select properties you want to display.
3. Click OK , when you are done.

**Case Study**

Let’s say, we need to show *Warning Order* and *Medical Condition* exchange elements that are produced and consumed by the performer *Search Node*. Exchange elements are information indirectly related to the *Search Node* element.

We will analyze the OV-2 diagram from the DoDAF sample project.

First, we will show the exchange elements produced by the*Search Node*.

Let’s create the **Produces Exchange Element** derived property for the performer *Search Node*. Open the *Search Node* Specification window and in the **Traceability** property group click the **Create** button. In the **Expression** dialog, select the **Metachain Navigation**property group. Then specify the derived property name and the expression for the meta chain as it is shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the following figure, you can see that a new subgroup named **Other** is created in the Performer Specification window. Our created **Produces Exchange Element** derived property is placed under this subgroup. The **Produces Exchange Element** derived property has Warning Order and Medical Condition exchange elements as its values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Next, we need to display values of the **Produces Exchange Element** derived property for the *Search Node* performer in the OV-2 diagram.

A derived property is applicable to an element type, not the specific element. In this particular example, our created derived properties are applicable to all performers, not only to Search Node.

We can use a Note element wherein we can display information indirectly related to the Search Node. Let’s create a Note and connect it to the *Search Node* performer. Then click the **Edit Compartment**. In the **Compartment Edit** dialog, select the **Element Properties** tab. From the properties list, select to display the Produces Exchange Element property. After clicking the **OK** button, Warning Order and Medical Condition exchange elements are displayed in the Note.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Now, let’s create a generic table wherein we can also display information indirectly related to the *Search Node*, as well as to other performers. For that purpose, we select Performer as an**Element Type** and add existing performers to the table. Then we select which information to display as new columns. Previously created **Produces Exchange Element** derived property is also available to add as a new column through the **Show Columns** menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Now, let’s create a new derived property directly in the generic table. This property will show which exchange elements are consumed by the performer element. We can do that using the same **Expression** dialog and doing the same actions as described previously. In the following figure is the example of the **Consumes Exchange Element** derived property expression.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When the expression is specified, the newly created derived property is displayed as a new column.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>While working with your models, sometimes in the same diagram or table, you need to see information that is not directly related to an element. You can display that information in the following ways:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="display_on_note"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[In the Note]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="display_in_table"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[In the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties" /></ac:link> (as a new column)</li></ul><p>To display the information indirectly related to an element, you need to <ac:link ac:anchor="create_new"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create derived properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties" /></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462626 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00225: Displaying Measurement Values

- page_id: `170462626`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462626/Displaying+Measurement+Values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-11T14:50:28.664+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['edit-measurement-values', 'display-measurement-values']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

While working with diagrams, you can display and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Measurement Values' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] for the element in the:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Measurement Values' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Measurement Values' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

in_compartmentTo display measurement values in the compartment area of the element shape

1. Right-click the element, for which you want to display measurements.
2. Click Edit Compartments and select Measurements tab.
3. Select the measurements you want to display.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

In the Symbol Properties dialog, you can select to display the name of the measured element.

in_note_or_commentTo display measurement values in the note or comment

1. Create a note or comment for the element which measurements you want to display.
2. Right-click the note or comment and select Edit Compartments .
3. Choose Measurements tab and select the measurements you want to display.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

- Only measurements with predefined values can be displayed.
- You can also display indirectly related measurement values.

editTo edit measurement values

1. Select an element with the displayed measurement values you want to edit.
2. Double-click a measurement value you want to edit.
3. Type a new value.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>While working with diagrams, you can display and <ac:link ac:anchor="edit"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[edit measurement values]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying Measurement Values" /></ac:link> for the element in the:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="in_compartment"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[compartment area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying Measurement Values" /></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="in_note_or_comment"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[note or comment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying Measurement Values" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurement_values_displayed_on_note.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying Measurement Values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor"><ac:parameter ac:name="">in_compartment</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display measurement values in the compartment area of the element shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the element, for which you want to display measurements.</li><li>Click<strong> Edit Compartments </strong>and select <strong>Measurements</strong> tab.</li><li>Select the measurements you want to display.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the Symbol Properties dialog, you can select to display the name of the measured element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor"><ac:parameter ac:name="">in_note_or_comment</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display measurement values in the note or comment</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a note or comment for the element which measurements you want to display.</li><li>Right-click the note or comment and select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</li><li>Choose <strong>Measurements</strong> tab and select the measurements you want to display.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Only measurements with predefined values can be displayed.</li><li>You can also display indirectly related measurement values.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor"><ac:parameter ac:name="">edit</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To edit measurement values</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element with the displayed measurement values you want to edit.</li><li>Double-click a measurement value you want to edit.</li><li>Type a new value.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462150 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00226: Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from Cr diagram

- page_id: `170462150`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462150/Displaying+Sub+Capabilities+deleted+from+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T16:19:54.578+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['display-deleted-from-chart-in-cr', 'display-sub-capability-deleted-from-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The capability name becomes *Italic* when one or more sub capabilities are deleted from the chart.

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Display Deleted From Chart and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capability you want to be displayed.
  - Select Display All to display all deleted Sub Capabilities.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The capability name becomes <em>Italic</em> when one or more sub capabilities are deleted from the chart.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To display deleted Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Display Deleted From Chart</strong> and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Sub Capability you want to be displayed.</li><li>Select<strong> Display All</strong> to display all deleted Sub Capabilities.</li></ul></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461771 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00228: DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model

- page_id: `170461771`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461771/DIV-1+Conceptual+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:35:49.405+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-1-conceptual-data-model', 'div-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

638611895

638611898

638611897

**Description**

The DIV-1, a new DoDAF-described Model in DoDAF V2.0, addresses the information concepts at a high-level on an operational architecture.

The DIV-1 is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The intended usage of the DIV-1 includes:

- Information requirements.
- Information hierarchy.

**Implementation**

DIV-1 can be represented using a DIV-1 diagram which is based in the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The DIV-1 defines each kind of information classes associated with the Architectural Description scope, mission, or business as its own Entity, with its associated attributes and relationships. These Entity definitions correlate to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] information elements and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] inputs, outputs, and controls.

638611894

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Entity Attribute' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461774 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00229: DIV-2 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `170461774`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461774/DIV-2+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:13:15.868+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-2-logical-data-model', 'div-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639161871

639161873

639161872

**Description**

The DIV-2 allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level data elements are included in the descriptions.

**Implementation**

DIV-2 can be represented using:

- A DIV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='DIV-2.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

Data described in a DIV-2 may be related to Information in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] or and Activity Resource (where the Resource is Data) flow object in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']. This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.

The DIV-2 information entities and elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

The information entities and elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

639161869

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461777 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00230: DIV-3 Physical Data Model

- page_id: `170461777`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461777/DIV-3+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:10:36.185+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-3-physical-data-model', 'div-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639670783

639670786

639670784

**Description**

The DIV-3 defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. The Physical Schema is one of the models closest to actual system design in DoDAF. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the DIV-2 Logical Data Model is actually implemented.

While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

**Implementation**

DIV-3 can be represented using:

- A DIV-3 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='DIV-3.PNG']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition Diagram.

The Physical Data Model is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']) is actually implemented.

639670781

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Entity Item' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exchange Element' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461675 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00231: DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views

- page_id: `170461675`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461675/DoDAF+2.0+viewpoints+and+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:15:59.184+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: ['dodaf', 'department-of-defense-architecture-framework']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

564848754

564848757

564848756

The Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) is an architecture framework for the United States Department of Defense (DoD) that provides visualization infrastructure for specific stakeholders concerns through viewpoints organized by various views. These views are artifacts for visualizing, understanding and assimilating the broad scope and complexities of an architecture description through tabular, structural, behavioral, ontological, pictorial, temporal, graphical, probabilistic, or alternative conceptual means.

This Architecture Framework is uniquely suited to large systems with complex integration and interoperability challenges, and it is unique in its employment of "operational views." These views offer overview and details aimed at specific stakeholders within their domain and in interaction with other domains in which the system will operate.

The DoDAF provides a foundational framework for developing and representing architecture descriptions that ensure a common denominator for understanding, comparing, and integrating architectures across organizational, joint, and multinational boundaries. It establishes data element definitions, rules, and relationships and a baseline set of products for the consistent development of systems, integrated, or federated architectures. These architecture descriptions may include families of systems (FoS), systems of systems (SoS), and net-centric capabilities for inter-operating and interacting in the non-combat environment.

The purpose of DoDAF is to define concepts and models usable in DoD's six core processes:

1. Joint Capabilities Integration and Development (JCIDS)
2. Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE)
3. Defense Acquisition System (DAS)
4. Systems Engineering (SE)
5. Operational Planning (OPLAN)
6. Capability Portfolio Management (CPM)

The DoDAF process (the following figure) is only recommendable. You can choose the creation sequence of diagrams according to your needs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

564848753

**Related Pages:**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9daf03e9-70d5-4f63-a328-fb2585ddfa8d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">564848754</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6b3a3cc-2dcc-4aa5-933a-a50234a93fa2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">564848757</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e9132e8-30bb-4ff4-8411-18ebebb826ba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">564848756</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><div><p>The Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) is an architecture framework for the United States Department of Defense (DoD) that provides visualization infrastructure for specific stakeholders concerns through viewpoints organized by various views. These views are artifacts for visualizing, understanding and assimilating the broad scope and complexities of an architecture description through tabular, structural, behavioral, ontological, pictorial, temporal, graphical, probabilistic, or alternative conceptual means.</p><p>This Architecture Framework is uniquely suited to large systems with complex integration and interoperability challenges, and it is unique in its employment of &quot;operational views.&quot; These views offer overview and details aimed at specific stakeholders within their domain and in interaction with other domains in which the system will operate.</p><p>The DoDAF provides a foundational framework for developing and representing architecture descriptions that ensure a common denominator for understanding, comparing, and integrating architectures across organizational, joint, and multinational boundaries. It establishes data element definitions, rules, and relationships and a baseline set of products for the consistent development of systems, integrated, or federated architectures. These architecture descriptions may include families of systems (FoS), systems of systems (SoS), and net-centric capabilities for inter-operating and interacting in the non-combat environment.</p><p>The purpose of DoDAF is to define concepts and models usable in DoD's six core processes:</p></div><div><ol><li>Joint Capabilities Integration and Development (JCIDS)</li><li>Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE)</li><li>Defense Acquisition System (DAS)</li><li>Systems Engineering (SE)</li><li>Operational Planning (OPLAN)</li><li>Capability Portfolio Management (CPM)</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e414ad8-e171-49a8-916c-81ef877956af"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The DoDAF process (the following figure) is only recommendable. You can choose the creation sequence of diagrams according to your needs.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="DoDAF process" ac:alt="DoDAF process"><ri:attachment ri:filename="DoDAF_process_2.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a8d1558-c779-4035-95a6-1396f5847601"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">564848753</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="78e24942-5155-46ba-b0c9-08ab7e7f3e7a" /><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462281 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00232: Dr Deployment Schedule

- page_id: `170462281`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462281/Dr+Deployment+Schedule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T07:42:27.445+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Deployed Resources viewpoint
- labels: ['dr-deployment-schedule', 'dr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Dr Deployment Schedule view addresses the roll-out of resource packages (new capabilities, organizational change, etc.) into individual organizations. This view shows the planned capability deployment into individual organizations for a set of Enterprise Phases. Each Enterprise Phase snapshot lists the Organizations and the Resource Package that it is planned to receive. Each Resource Package may be traced to the Capability it delivers. The Dr view may also specify the possible interactions between the deployed Capability so that planners can see at a glance which organizations have compatible systems, and when.

The Dr view may be used for:

- Fielding planning.
- Business change planning.
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability options analysis.
- Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.
- Identification of deployment level shortfalls.

**Implementation**

the Dr view can be represented using:

- A Dr table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A Dr spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Deployed Milestone
- No Longer Used Milestone
- Actual Project
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The Dr view may also specify the possible interactions between the deployed Capability so that planners can see at a glance which organizations have compatible systems, and when.</p><p>The Dr view may <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462601 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00233: Dragging and Dropping

- page_id: `170462601`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462601/Dragging+and+Dropping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T12:28:16.758+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['drag', 'drop', 'select-drag-drop-rule', 'drag-and-drop']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Create relationships or assign property values by dragging and dropping one element onto another right in the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

There are a number of predefined rules to create relationships or assign property values automatically. You may also set up your own drag-and-drop specifications between any elements.

To select a rule

1. Do one of the following:
  - Drag a selected element onto another, hold it for a few seconds and then release it. A shortcut menu is displayed.
  - Right-click an element and drag it onto another (This action is valid only for Windows operating system users). A shortcut menu is displayed.
2. From the shortcut menu, select one of the rules you want to apply.

The following table lists the predefined drag and drop rules. There rules define the possible results when dragging source element on the target element.

| Source Element | Target Element | Created Relationship | Assigned Property |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Organization | Project | Organizational Project Relationship |  |
| Service AccessPhysical ArchitectureCapability Configuration | NodePerformer | Implements |  |

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| endDate : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The time and date at which the Phase ends. |
| startDate : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The time and date at which the Phase starts. |
| goals : EnterpriseGoal[*] | The Goal towards which this Phase is directed and is in support of. |
| visions : EnterpriseVision[*] | The Vision towards which this Phase is directed and is in support of. |
| statementTasks : EnduringTask[*] | Collection of statement tasks. |
| fulfills : Mission [*] |  |
| describedBy : ArchitecturalDescription [*] |  |

## PAGE 00242: Environment

- page_id: `170462321`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462321/Environment

- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097168225

1097168227

1097168226

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A definition of the conditions in which something exists or functions. An Environment may be specified in terms of LocationType (e.g., terrain), Climate (e.g., tropical), and LightCondition (e.g., dark, light, dusk, etc.).

DoDAF: An object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related DoDAF views

[CV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/CV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+StV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

##### Related NAF views

[NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NCV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462515 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00244: Environment Selection dialog

- page_id: `170462515`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462515/Environment+Selection+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-03T08:53:35.328+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['environment-selection-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Environment Selection** dialog opens when starting the UPDM plugin for the first time.

To select the enterprise architecture framework environment

1. Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build: Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog (see the following figure). This information may help you decide what to choose.
  - DoDAF 2.0
  - MODAF
  - NAF v. 3
  - NAF 4.0
2. Click to select the Expert check box, if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the Expert check box unselected.
3. Click the Continue button. The sample project corresponding the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can change the selected environment and its mode via the **Select Perspective** dialog.

To open **Select Perspective** dialog

1. Click Options > Perspectives > Perspectives . The Select Perspective dialog opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Environment Selection</strong> dialog opens when starting the UPDM plugin for the first time.</p><p>To select the enterprise architecture framework environment</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build:<br /><ul><li>DoDAF 2.0</li><li>MODAF</li><li>NAF v. 3</li><li>NAF 4.0</li></ul>Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog (see the following figure). This information may help you decide what to choose.</li><li>Click to select the <strong>Expert</strong> check box, if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the <strong>Expert</strong> check box unselected.</li><li>Click the <strong>Continue</strong> button. The sample project corresponding the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:alt="Environment Selection dialog" ac:title="Environment Selection dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_selection_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Environment Selection dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change the selected environment and its mode via the <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog.</p><p>To open <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong>.<br />The <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

##### Description

UPDM: Relationship between a Node and a capability the node provides.

MODAF/NAF: (MODAF::CapabilityForNode): An assertion that a Node is required to have a Capability.

DoDAF: A couple that represents the capability that a performer manifests.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency
