<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462640 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00247: Exporting tables to Excel spreadsheets

- page_id: `170462640`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462640/Exporting+tables+to+Excel+spreadsheets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-26T13:09:31.465+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Tables
- labels: ['export-tables-to-excel-spreadsheets']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export your active OV-3, NOV-3, OV-6a, NOV-6a, AV-2, NAV-2. SV-6, NSV-6, SV-10a, NSV-10a, SOV-4a, StV-5, CV-5, SvcV-10a, SV-7 Actual, SV-7 Typical, NSV-7 Actual, NSV-7 Typical, SvcV7-Actual, and SvcV-7 Typical tables into the Excel Spreadsheet.

To export a table

1. On the table toolbar, click the Export button.
2. In the Choose file dialog, specify the file name and location.
3. Click Save . The table is exported to Excel spreadsheet.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can export your active OV-3, NOV-3, OV-6a, NOV-6a, AV-2, NAV-2. SV-6, NSV-6, SV-10a, NSV-10a, SOV-4a, StV-5, CV-5, SvcV-10a, SV-7 Actual, SV-7 Typical, NSV-7 Actual, NSV-7 Typical, SvcV7-Actual, and SvcV-7 Typical tables into the Excel Spreadsheet.</p><p>To export a table</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Export</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Choose </strong><strong>file</strong> dialog, specify the file name and location.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>.<br />The table is exported to Excel spreadsheet.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_table-to_excel.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting tables to Excel spreadsheets" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462603 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00248: Exporting to PES

- page_id: `170462603`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462603/Exporting+to+PES
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T12:31:08.545+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['pes', 'export-model', 'export-view', 'export-viewpoint', 'export-to-pes', 'physical-exchange-specification']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Physical Exchange Specification is the specification for architectural data exchange.

You can export your model, view, or viewpoint to the Physical Exchange Specification (PES) file. This export makes it easier to work with architectural data of your model, view, or viewpoint in another environment (for example, you will be able to analyze exported data using spreadsheets or deposit information into repositories). The PES export is based on the DM2 and IDEAS Foundation XML schemas.

- You can find the IDEAS Foundation XML schema on the International Defense Enterprise Architecture Specification Group official Web site at http://www.ideasgroup.org/xsd/IdeasFoundation.xsd
- You can find the MD2 Foundation XML schema on the Department of Defense Chief Information Officer official Web site at http://cio-nii.defense.gov/sites/dodaf20/products/DM2Foundation_v2-01.xsd
- You can download the MD2 Domain XML schema at [http://metadata.ces.mil/dse/ns/ARCH/DM2_PES_v2.02_Chg_1.zip](http://metadata.ces.mil/dse/ns/ARCH/DM2_PES_v2.02_Chg_1.zip)

Elements and relations from the model, view, or viewpoint are exported to the selected location. The export is carried out using the QVT scripts. As MagicDraw supports QVT, you can easily modify the necessary script. You can find all related QVT scripts in <MagicDraw installation directory>\plugins\com.nomagic.updm\PES\qvtscripts\export. 
 You can download the QVT plugin from [http://www.nomagic.com/products/magicdraw-addons/qvt-plugin](http://www.nomagic.com/products/magicdraw-addons/qvt-plugin). For more information about the MagicDraw QVT plugin, see "QVT_plugin_readme.html" that can be found in <MagicDraw installation directory> and "SimpleUML_to_RDB_Sample_readme.html" that can be found in <MagicDraw installation directory>\samples\QVT. 
 
 To export a model

1. On the **File**menu, point to **Export****To**, and click **PES****(v2.02)****File**.
2. In the **Select PES File** dialog, specify the location and name of the PES file.
3. Click **Export**.

To export a view or a viewpoint

1. Right-click the view or viewpoint and select Export To PES .
2. In the **Select PES File** dialog, specify the location and name of the PES file.
3. Click **Export**.

- All exported data is stored in the XML file.
- After exporting to PES, predefined element ID's are not changed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Physical Exchange Specification is the specification for architectural data exchange.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> You can export your model, view, or viewpoint to the Physical Exchange Specification (PES) file. This export makes it easier to work with architectural data of your model, view, or viewpoint in another environment (for example, you will be able to analyze exported data using spreadsheets or deposit information into repositories). The PES export is based on the DM2 and IDEAS Foundation XML schemas.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="41147e4c-ca6e-41ed-8b29-db8712d222b2" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can find the IDEAS Foundation XML schema on the International Defense Enterprise Architecture Specification Group official Web site at <a href="http://www.ideasgroup.org/xsd/IdeasFoundation.xsd">http://www.ideasgroup.org/xsd/IdeasFoundation.xsd</a></li><li>You can find the MD2 Foundation XML schema on the Department of Defense Chief Information Officer official Web site at <a href="http://cio-nii.defense.gov/sites/dodaf20/products/DM2Foundation_v2-01.xsd">http://cio-nii.defense.gov/sites/dodaf20/products/DM2Foundation_v2-01.xsd</a><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span></span></li><li>You can download the MD2 Domain XML schema <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3a4b98c7-d4a3-41ce-87ab-f8dfa32ead15">at <a href="http://metadata.ces.mil/dse/ns/ARCH/DM2_PES_v2.02_Chg_1.zip">http://metadata.ces.mil/dse/ns/ARCH/DM2_PES_v2.02_Chg_1.zip</a></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Elements and relations from the model, view, or viewpoint are exported to the selected location. The export is carried out using the QVT scripts. As MagicDraw supports QVT, you can easily modify the necessary script. You can find all related QVT scripts in &lt;MagicDraw installation directory&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.updm\PES\qvtscripts\export. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> You can download the QVT plugin from <span class="nolink"><a href="http://www.nomagic.com/products/magicdraw-addons/qvt-plugin">http://www.nomagic.com/products/magicdraw-addons/qvt-plugin</a></span>. For more information about the MagicDraw QVT plugin, see &quot;QVT_plugin_readme.html&quot; that can be found in  &lt;MagicDraw installation directory&gt; and &quot;SimpleUML_to_RDB_Sample_readme.html&quot; that can be found in &lt;MagicDraw installation directory&gt;\samples\QVT. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To export a model</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">On the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>File</strong> </span>menu, point to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Export</strong><strong> To</strong></span>, and click <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>PES</strong><strong>(v2.02)</strong><strong>File</strong></span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Select PES File</strong></span> dialog, specify the location and name of the PES file. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Export</strong>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> To export a view or a viewpoint</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the view or viewpoint and select <strong>Export</strong><strong> To</strong><strong> PES</strong>.</li><li>In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Select PES File</strong></span> dialog, specify the location and name of the PES file.</li><li><p>Click <strong>Export</strong>.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="3942c8b3-3949-4a79-afdb-6a45d553be1a" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>All exported data is stored in the XML file.</li><li>After exporting to PES, predefined element ID's are not changed.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462604 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00252: Filtering Operational Activities and Functions

- page_id: `170462604`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462604/Filtering+Operational+Activities+and+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-26T15:10:01.109+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['filter-options', 'filter-operational-activities', 'filter-functions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to filter the activities and functions according to your needs in the:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Activities dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Producing and Consuming Activities** dialog .

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog.

****

**Filtering Operational Activities**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Filter Option | Filter allows the selection of... |
| --- | --- |
| Performed Activities | The Activities that are performed by the Node. |
| Show all Operational Activities composing performed Operational Activities | The Activities that are the composite parts of directly performed Activities. |
| Show only TOP most Operational Activities | TOP most Activities. |
| Show only atomic Operational Activities | The Activities that are composite parts of other Activities and do not have composite parts. |

****

**Filtering Functions**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Filter Option | Filter allows the selection of... |
| --- | --- |
| Performed Functions | The Functions that are performed by the Resource. |
| Show all Functions/ System Functions composing performed Functions | The Functions that are the composite parts of directly performed Functions. |
| Show only TOP most Functions | TOP most Functions. |
| Show only atomic Function | The Functions that are composite parts of other Functions and do not have composite parts. |

To apply filters

- Select appropriate options from the drop-down list.

For the more concrete element selection, use different combinations of these filters.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Activities dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

MODAF/NAF: A statement about the future state of one or more types of system or standard.

| --- | --- |

## PAGE 00254: Function

- page_id: `170462452`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462452/Function

- version_date: `2024-05-08T13:09:01.520+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

315011046

315011048

315011047

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An activity which is specified in context of the resource (human or machine) that performs it.

DoDAF: Activity: Work, not specific to a single organization, weapon system or individual that transforms inputs (Resources) into outputs (Resources) or changes their state.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| subject : ResourceInteractionItem[*] | The ResourceInteractionItem that is the subject of the Function. |

##### Extensions

UML Activity

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/MODAF.+SV-4+Functionality+Description)

[MODAF. SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/MODAF.+SV-5+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix)

[MODAF. SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/MODAF.+SV-10a+Resource+Constraints+Specification)

##### Related NAF views

[NSV-4 System Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/NSV-4+System+Functionality+Description)

[NSV-5 System Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/NSV-5+System+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix)

[NSV-10a Systems Rule Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NSV-10a+Systems+Rule+Model)

## PAGE 00255: Function Action

- page_id: `170462453`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462453/Function+Action
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T13:09:01.663+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

471485503

471485505

471485504

##### Description

UPDM Artifact: The FunctionAction is defined as a call behavior action that invokes the function that needs to be performed. This concept is required for mapping the architecture with UML and does not have a DoDAF, MODAF, or NAF equivalent.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Call Behavior Action

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/MODAF.+SV-4+Functionality+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NSV-4 System Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/NSV-4+System+Functionality+Description)

- page_id: `170462454`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`

- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Availability

472416544

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Availability

472416546

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Availability

472416545

##### Description

UPDM: An extension of «ActivityEdge» that is used to model the flow of control/objects through a Function.

MODAF/NAF: A FunctionEdge (MODAF::FunctionFlow, NAF::FunctionFlow) is a UML::ObjectFlow between Functions.

#### INFO: Availability

Availability

This has been extended in UPDM to additionally include UML::ControlFlows.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Activity Edge

##### Related DoDAF views

[SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/MODAF.+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow)

[MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/MODAF.+SV-4+Functionality+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+Definition)

[NSV-4 System Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/NSV-4+System+Functionality+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462291 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00259: Generating DARS report in A1

- page_id: `170462291`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462291/Generating+DARS+report+in+A1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-04T06:29:57.299+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > A1 Meta-Data Definitions
- labels: ['dars-template', 'generate-dars-report-in-a1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before generating an A1 DARS report, you must load the DARS template in A1 diagram and provide the template with relevant data.

To load the DARS template in A1

1. Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.
2. Click the Load DARS Template button to load DARS template. [ATTACHMENT filename='DARS_template.png']
3. Fill in the extension property values for the A1 DARS report. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Before generating an A1 DARS report, you must load the DARS template in A1 diagram and provide the template with relevant data.<span> </span></p><p>To load the DARS template in A1</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.</li><li>Click the <strong>Load DARS Template</strong> button to load DARS template.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="DARS_template.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in A1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Fill in the extension property values for the A1 DARS report.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Architectural_description_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in A1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></li></ol><p> </p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To generate DARS report use standard <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link> procedure.</span></span></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170461679 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00260: Generating DARS report in AV-1

- page_id: `170461679`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170461679/Generating+DARS+report+in+AV-1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-04T06:29:41.823+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > All Views viewpoint > AV-1 Overview and Summary Information
- labels: ['dars-template', 'generate-dars-report-in-av-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before generating an AV-1 DARS report, you must load the DARS template in AV-1 diagram and provide the template with relevant data.

To load the DARS template in AV-1

1. Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.
2. Click the Load DARS Template button to load DARS template. [ATTACHMENT filename='load_DARS_template_button_in_AV1.png']
3. Fill in the extension property values for the AV-1 DARS report. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To generate DARS report use standard [Generating document based reports](http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDMP/Generating+document+based+reports) procedure.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Before generating an AV-1 DARS report, you must load the DARS template in AV-1 diagram and provide the template with relevant data.<span> </span></p><p>To load the DARS template in AV-1</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.</li><li>Click the <strong>Load DARS Template</strong> button to load DARS template.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="load_DARS_template_button_in_AV1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in AV-1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Fill in the extension property values for the AV-1 DARS report.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Architectural_description_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in AV-1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span><br /><br /></li></ol><p>T<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">o generate DARS report use standard <a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDMP/Generating+document+based+reports">Generating document based reports</a> procedure.</span></span></span></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462608 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00261: Generating document based reports

- page_id: `170462608`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462608/Generating+document+based+reports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T12:37:12.420+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Reports
- labels: ['create-report', 'quick-generate-report', 'report-wizard', 'generate-document-based-reports']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw report handling functionality allows you to store UPDM report data in a UPDM project. With this feature you can easily manage multiple reports with different properties and quickly print a report directly from the model. Another benefit from storing your report data within a project is that you can easily pass the report on to other users by passing the project. 
 UPDM reports can be handled similarly as any other artifacts: diagrams, matrices, and tables. There is a report data element created by default in a project template for each UPDM report. A report data element is filled in with predefined default data. 
 You can create a new report data element from the shortcut menu of a viewpoint or view package. 
 
 To create an AV-1 Overview & Summary report data element

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the All Views package.
2. Select Create Report > AV-1 Overview & Summary Information . A new report data element that stores the default data for a particular report is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The newly created report data element is filled in with default data for successful generation of the report. To see variables and data of the report, expand the report element node in the Containment tree.

If you need to change data stored within a variable, open the variable's Specification window and then edit the **Value** property value. 
 
 You can store not only variables as data within a report data element, but the editable default report properties as well. The following table lists available editable default report properties and their descriptions.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | To specify the name of the report data element. |
| Generate Recursively | Set to true to generate the report, recursively including all inner packages within a defined report scope. |
| Data | To select one or more packages and / or elements to define a scope for generating the report. |
| Auto Image Size | To specify an image size in the report. |
| Empty Text | To specify a value to be inserted when data contains no value. |
| Image Format | To select an image format for all the images in the report. |
| Template | To select a report template.A report template is not part of a project. |
| Variables | To add, remove, and order report input variables. |

To edit a report property, open the report data element's Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 You can generate a report directly from the report data element without using the **Report** **Wizard**.

To generate a report from the report data element

1. Right-click a report data element and click **Quick** **Generate** **Report**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Specify the location to save the generated report.
3. Select whether or not to open the generated report directly after the generation.

You can create several reports with different data for each type of the report.

You can also use the MagicDraw **Report** **Wizard** for report generation. Using this wizard, you can generate a report either from existing report data elements (after loading them to the wizard) or from external report data.

To open the **Report** **Wizard**

- Click Tools > Report Wizard and select the report templates for the UPDM views.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw report handling functionality allows you to store UPDM report data in a UPDM project. With this feature you can easily manage multiple reports with different properties and quickly print a report directly from the model. Another benefit from storing your report data within a project is that you can easily pass the report on to other users by passing the project. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> UPDM reports can be handled similarly as any other artifacts: diagrams, matrices, and tables. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462237 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00262: Generating P1 Actual table from P1 Typical table

- page_id: `170462237`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462237/Generating+P1+Actual+table+from+P1+Typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T13:32:00.552+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['evaluate-measurements-in-p1', 'generate-p1-actual-table-from-p1-typical-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] can be instantiated by creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

To generate a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] from a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

1. Create a P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements Description table with at least one measurable Resource added.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Evaluate Measurements button. A new P1 Actual table is created.
3. Add actual values for all Actual Measurements in the P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements Description table. For each Measurement in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] , an Actual Measurement is created and the row representing it is added into the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b7c62d8f-6bdf-48f2-b623-dbe954e9c74e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can be instantiated by creating <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Actual table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To generate a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Actual table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements Description table with at least one measurable Resource added.</li><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Evaluate Measurements</strong> button. A new P1 Actual table is created.</li><li>Add actual values for all Actual Measurements in the P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements Description table.<br />For each Measurement in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, an Actual Measurement is created and the row representing it is added into the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Actual table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462647 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00268: Impact analysis relation maps

- page_id: `170462647`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462647/Impact+analysis+relation+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T10:52:47.473+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Traceability and Impact Analysis
- labels: ['impact-analysis-relation-maps', 'node-role-impact-analysis-map', 'node-impact-analysis-map', 'resource-role-impact-analysis-map', 'resource-impact-analysis-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Take your model to the next steps - perform the impact analysis. For this purpose, there are four predefined relation maps. Using these maps, you are able to identify the model elements which are affected by the changes of the context element.

The predefined relation maps for the impact analysis are the following:

- Node Role Impact Analysis Map
- Node Impact Analysis Map
- Resource Role Impact Analysis Map
- Resource Impact Analysis Map

Several predefined criteria helps you to determine how the context element is related to other model elements.

The Legend in the right-top corner shows you which color represents which criteria.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating predefined relation map
- Modifying criteria through the legend

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Take your model to the next steps - perform the impact analysis. For this purpose, there are four predefined relation maps. Using these maps, you are able to identify the model elements which are affected by the changes of the context element.</p><p>The predefined relation maps for the impact analysis are the following:</p><ul><li>Node Role Impact Analysis Map</li><li>Node Impact Analysis Map</li><li>Resource Role Impact Analysis Map</li><li>Resource Impact Analysis Map</li></ul><p>Several predefined criteria helps you to determine how the context element is related to other model elements.</p><p>The Legend in the right-top corner shows you which color represents which criteria.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Impact analysis relation maps" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/Creating+predefined+relation+map">Creating predefined relation map</a></li><li><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/Modifying+criteria+through+the+legend">Modifying criteria through the legend</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462682 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00269: Implementation Matrix

- page_id: `170462682`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462682/Implementation+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T13:58:03.788+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['implementation-matrix', 'mapping-between-systems-operational-and-service-elements', 'nodes/performers-implementation-matrix', 'operational-activities-implementation-matrix', 'operational-exchanges-implementation-matrix', 'ov-sv-gap-analysis']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Implementation Matrix describes the mapping between Systems Operational and Service oriented viewpoint elements.

The DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF Operational viewpoint elements should be implemented by the Systems or service oriented viewpoint elements. The implementation in UPDM is defined by the Implements relationship connecting implementation and specification elements In order to specify the implementation and analyze implementation gaps the Implementation Matrix is added as a supportive product for UPDM.

**Implementation**

An Implementation Matrix in MagicDraw can be represented by a Dependency Matrix based diagram. The implementation elements will be used as the row elements and specification (implemented) elements will be used as the column elements.

To be more specific, systems elements are: System Resources, Functions, and Resource Interactions.

Operational elements are: Nodes (MODAF / NAF) or Performers (DoDAF), Operational Activities, and Operational Exchanges.

Service oriented elements are: Service Function.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**Predefined configurations**

There are several types of predefined implementation matrices:

- Nodes/Performers Implementation Matrix. It maps Nodes/Performers to System Resources only.
- Operational Activities Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Activities to Functions.
- Operational Exchanges Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Exchanges to Resource Interactions.

You can find predefined matrices by clicking **Analyze** > **OV-SV Gap analysis**.

These matrices provide analysis of the whole model of a particular implementation, so you do not have to define the scope or any additional properties for building them.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Function
- Resource Interaction
- Node
- Performer
- Operational Activity
- Operational Exchange
- System Function

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Implementation Matrix describes the mapping between Systems Operational and Service oriented viewpoint elements.</p><p>The DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF Operational viewpoint elements should be implemented by the Systems or service oriented viewpoint elements. The implementation in UPDM is defined by the Implements relationship connecting implementation and specification elements In order to specify the implementation and analyze implementation gaps the Implementation Matrix is added as a supportive product for UPDM.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>An Implementation Matrix in MagicDraw can be represented by a Dependency Matrix based diagram. The implementation elements will be used as the row elements and specification (implemented) elements will be used as the column elements.</p><p>To be more specific, systems elements are: System Resources, Functions, and Resource Interactions.</p><p>Operational elements are: Nodes (MODAF / NAF) or Performers (DoDAF), Operational Activities, and Operational Exchanges.</p><p>Service oriented elements are: Service Function.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:alt="Implementation Matrix" ac:title="Implementation Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Implementation_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Implementation Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><p> </p><p><strong>Predefined configurations</strong></p><p>There are several types of predefined implementation matrices:</p><ul><li>Nodes/Performers Implementation Matrix. It maps Nodes/Performers to System Resources only.</li><li>Operational Activities Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Activities to Functions.</li><li>Operational Exchanges Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Exchanges to Resource Interactions.</li></ul><p>You can find predefined matrices by clicking <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>OV-SV Gap analysis</strong>.</p><p>These matrices provide analysis of the whole model of a particular implementation, so you do not have to define the scope or any additional properties for building them.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="dea06eaf-b519-435c-9eae-f05f7ce4521d" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="2bcf9555-1f9c-4da6-a8b5-9e9b1e3833a0" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="55d28ffb-939e-409f-85c8-61b1989962d6" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>System</li><li>Function</li><li>Resource Interaction</li><li>Node</li><li>Performer</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Operational Exchange</li><li>System Function</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462614 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00274: Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges

- page_id: `170462614`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462614/Inheritance+Aggregation+Composition+and+Redefinition+of+Exchanges
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T13:34:24.801+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['inheritance-of-exchanges', 'aggregation-of-exchanges', 'composition-of-exchanges', 'redefinition-of-exchanges', 'aggregated-information', 'display-all-operational-exchanges', 'hide-inherited-exchanges', 'see-inherited-exchanges', 'redefine-operational-exchange']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Exchanges and Resource Interactions can be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.

You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- Role-based tables [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] , NOV-3, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] , NSV-6, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L3 Node Interactions' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] , and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P3 Resource Connectivity' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
**Case Study**

Let's say, we have performers *Search**Node* and *Place**Of**Safety* related with the Operational Exchange *Warning**Order*. The performer *Aircraft* specifies performer *Search**Node*. Then we draw an association between *Aircraft* and *Place**of**Safety*. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized or not and also can be redefined.

Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:

- By clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='display_all_operational_exchanges.png'] in the association's smart manipulator toolbar.
- By selecting the check box in the Realize column in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UPDM .
3. Set the Respect UPDM Information Flows on Generalization property value to false .

- Note, that the exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.

All the inherited exchanges can also be seen in the role-based tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray. You can use the Inherited Operational Exchange or Resource Interaction option to see the inherited exchanges.

To see the inherited exchanges

1. From the active table's toolbar, select Show Rows and then click Inherited Operational Exchange or Resource Interaction button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To redefine the Operational Exchange

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog , select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the Redefine button. The new Operational Exchange is created.

All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Concept Role
- Node
- Performer
- System Resource
- Operational Exchange
- Resource Interaction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can redefine the inherited flows.</p><p>You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Operational <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462619 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00275: Instantiating Structures

- page_id: `170462619`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462619/Instantiating+Structures
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T10:56:18.571+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard', 'create-instance', 'instantiate-proposed-typical-structure', 'typica-updm-element', 'actual-updm-element', 'instant-structures']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard to instantiate any typical structure of UPDM architecture automatically.

To open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UPDM elements and right-click them.
2. On the shortcut menu, choose Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 To instantiate the proposed typical structure

1. Select all typical classes you want to instantiate in a diagram pane or in the Model Browser.
2. Right-click on the selected typical UPDM elements and select **Tools** > **Create****Instance**.
3. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done. A new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] Actual diagram is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For instantiable UPDM elements corresponding actual elements are created. For example, by instantiating Organization results in Actual Organization and etc. For all other structural elements UML Instance Specifications are created.

| Typical UPDM element | Actual UPDM element |
| --- | --- |
| Project Type | Project (DoDAF), Actual Project (MODAF / NAF) |
| Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone |
| Project Theme | Project Status |
| Project Milestone Role | Actual Project Milestone Role |
| Organization | Actual Organization |
| Organization Type | Organization |
| Sub Organization (Resource Role typed by and owned by Organization Type (DoDAF) orOrganization (MODAF / NAF)) | Actual Organization Role |
| Person Type | Individual Person Role |
| Post | Actual Post |
| Post Role (Resource Role typed by Person Type (DoDAF) orPost (MODAF / NAF) and owned by Organization Type (DoDAF)or Organization (MODAF / NAF)). | Actual Organization Role |
| Person | Actual Person |
| Measurement Set | Actual Measurement Set |
| Measure Type | Measure |
| Measurement | Actual Measurement |
| Geo Political Extent Type | Geo Political Extent |
| Location Type | Actual Location |
| Location | Actual Location |
| Capability Configuration | Fielded Capability |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

1102400360

1102400362

1102400361

##### Description

UPDM: Links a Performer to the behavior that it can perform.

DoDAF: The Performs (DoDAF::activityPerformedByPerformer) relationship is an overlap between a Performer and a PerformedActivity (DoDAF::Activity) wherein the activity is performed by the Performer.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF

##### Extension

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462154 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00278: L1 Node Types

- page_id: `170462154`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462154/L1+Node+Types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:25:59.948+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l1-node-types', 'l1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L1 Node Types view specifies the types of logical Node used in the architecture. The nodes can be organized into a specialization hierarchy (subtype-supertype). An L1 view is used to define all the Nodes that will appear in a Logical Architecture. Nodes are elements of capability that are assembled and orchestrated in Logical Architecture. The levels of capability provided by each node are expressed as Measures of Performance, and these may be dependent on the environments in which the Node is required to operate.

The L1 view may be used to:

- Initial set up of a Logical Architecture.
- Define measure of performance for requirements specification purposes.
- Define the types of environment in which Nodes may operate.

**Implementation**

To create an L1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:

- L1 Node Types diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L1 Free FormNode Types diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L1 Node Types diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']Creating L1 Node Types diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']Creating L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Node
- Logical Architecture
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Node Port
- Capability
- Exhibits
- Actual Location
- Location Type
- Energy
- Materiel
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Operational Activity
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Problem Domain
- Security Domain

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The L1 Node Types view specifies the types of logical Node used in the architecture. The nodes can be organized into a specialization hierarchy (subtype-supertype). An L1 view is used to define all the Nodes that will appear in a Logical Architecture. Nodes are elements of capability that are assembled and orchestrated in Logical Architecture. The levels of capability provided by each node are expressed as Measures of Performance, and these may be dependent on the environments in which the Node is required to operate.</p><p>The L1 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Initial set up of a Logical Architecture.</li><li>Define measure of performance for requirements specification purposes.</li><li>Define the types of environment in which Nodes may operate.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>To create an L1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link">L1 Node Types <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462160 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00279: L2 Logical Scenario

- page_id: `170462160`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462160/L2+Logical+Scenario
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:24:42.218+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l2-logical-scenario', 'l2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L2 Logical Scenario view shows, at a high level, the interactions between logical nodes and places them in the context of location(s). The primary purpose of the L2 view is to specify nodes (elements of capability) in context of each other. The context is usually expressed in terms of the information that flows between the nodes (e.g. the information flow requirements between capabilities in a given scenario). The context may also be flows of material, human resource or energy.

The L2 view may be used to:

- Define operational concepts.
- Elaborate capability requirements.
- Define collaboration needs.
- Associate capability with a location.
- Define problem space.
- Operational planning.
- Supply chain analysis.

**Implementation**

The L2 view can be represented using an L2 diagram which is based on theUML Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Node Port
- Node Role
- Known Resource
- Needline
- Service Channel
- Service
- Property
- Request

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462164 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00280: L3 Node Interactions

- page_id: `170462164`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462164/L3+Node+Interactions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-12-11T12:32:47.228+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l3-node-interactions', 'l3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L3 Node Interactions view addresses operational information exchanges between nodes. Information exchanges provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on information exchanges that cross the capability boundary.

Although the primary purpose of this view is to specify information exchanges, an L3 may also list flows of materiel, energy and human resources.

The L3 view may be used to:

- Define operational concepts.
- Elaborate capability requirements.
- Define collaboration needs.
- Associate capability with a location.
- Define Problem space.
- Operational planning.
- Analyze Supply chain.
- Secure models – e.g. domain-based security & entity trust models.

**Implementation**

The L3 view can be represented using:

- An L3 Node Interactions table. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- An L3 Node Role Interactions table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L3_node_role_interactions.png']

- An L3 spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L3 Node Interactions table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Node
- Node Role
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel
- Operational Activity

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462168 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00281: L4 Logical Activities

- page_id: `170462168`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462168/L4+Logical+Activities
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:25:08.829+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l4-logical-activities', 'l4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L4 Logical Activities view describes the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal. It describes operational activities (or tasks), Input/Output flows between activities and to/from activities that are outside the scope of the Architecture. An operational activity is a logical process, specified independently of how it is carried out. To maintain this independence from implementation, logical nodes in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] are used to represent the structure which carries out the operational activities. Operational activities are realized as functions (P4) which are the “how” to the Operational Activities’ “what”. That is, they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The L4 activity models describe the business processes associated with the architecture, as well as the:

- Relationships or dependencies among the business processes.
- Information exchanged between business processes.
- External interchanges (from/to business processes that are outside the scope of the model).

The L4 view may be used to: 
 • Clearly delineate lines of responsibility for activities when coupled with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']. 
 • Uncover unnecessary Operational Activity redundancy. 
 • Make decisions about streamlining, combining, or omitting activities. 
 • Define or flag issues, opportunities, or operational activities and their interactions (information flows among the activities) that need to be scrutinised further. 
 • Provide a necessary foundation for depicting activity sequencing and timing in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L8 Logical Constraints' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L5 Logical States' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L6 Logical Sequence' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

**Implementation**

The L4 view can be represented using:

- An L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity Flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L4_flow_model.png']

- An L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L4.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational Activity
- Operational Parameter
- Standard Operational Activity
- Node
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Operational Activity Action
- Operational Activity Edge
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462174 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00282: L5 Logical States

- page_id: `170462174`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462174/L5+Logical+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:25:40.300+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l5-logical-states', 'l5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L5 Logical States view describes how operational nodes or activities respond to various events by changing state. The view represents the sets of events to which the architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The L5 view can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The L5 is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'].

**Implementation**

The L5 view can be represented using an UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational State Description
- Operational State
- Node

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The view represents the sets of events to which the architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The L5 view can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The L5 is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4 - Logical Activities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L5 view can be represented using an UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="L5 Logical States" ac:title="L5 Logical States"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="L5 Logical States" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational State Description</li><li>Operational State</li><li>Node</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462176 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00283: L6 Logical Sequence

- page_id: `170462176`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462176/L6+Logical+Sequence
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:26:37.889+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l6-logical-sequence', 'l6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L6 Logical Sequence view provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating Operational Nodes as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

Operational Event-Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of interactions between nodes in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The node interactions may correspond to flows of information, energy, material or people specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']. The L6 can be used by itself or in conjunction with an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L5 Logical States' space='UPDM2P2024xR1'] to describe the dynamic behavior of nodes. The diagram below shows the components of an L6. The items across the top of the diagram are operational nodes.

Each node has a vertical time-line associated with it. Specific points in time can be labeled running down the left-hand side of the diagram. Directed lines between the node time lines represent interactions (e.g. information exchanges) between nodes, and the points at which they intersect the timeliness represent the times at which the nodes become aware of the events.

The L6 view may be used to:

- Analyze operational events.
- Sequences of interactions between nodes.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identify non-functional user requirements (input to URD).
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

The L6 view can be represented using a L6 diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational Event Trace
- Operational Message
- Operational Exchange
- Node
- Node Role

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>Operational Event-Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of interactions between nodes in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The node interactions may correspond to flows of information, energy, material or people specified in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="L2 Logical Scenario" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L2 - Logical Scenario]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The L6 can be used by itself or in conjunction with an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="L5 Logical States" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L5 - Logical States]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of nodes. The diagram below shows the components of an L6. The items across the top of the diagram are operational nodes.</p><p>Each node has a vertical time-line associated with it. Directed lines between the node time lines represent interactions (e.g. information exchanges) between nodes, and the points at which they intersect the timeliness represent the times at which the nodes become aware of the events.</p><p>The L6 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Analyze operational events.</li><li>Sequences of interactions between nodes.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identify non-functional user requirements (input to URD).</li><li>Operational test scenarios.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L6 view can be represented using a L6 diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="L6 Logical Sequence diagram" ac:title="L6 Logical Sequence diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L6.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="L6 Logical Sequence" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Event Trace</li><li>Operational Message</li><li>Operational Exchange</li><li>Node</li><li>Node Role</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462179 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00284: L7 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `170462179`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462179/L7+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:26:52.890+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l7-logical-data-model', 'l7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L7 Logical Data Model view addresses the information perspective on an operational architecture. The L7 view is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information exchanges of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The L7 view may be used for:

- Information architecture.
- Information product hierarchy.

**Implementation**

The L7 view can be represented using:

- An L7 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L7.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L7 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Logical Data Model
- Entity Item
- Entity Attribute
- Entity Relationship
- Details
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462182 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00285: L8 Logical Constraints

- page_id: `170462182`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462182/L8+Logical+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:27:43.589+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l8-logical-constraints', 'l8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L8 Operational Rules Model specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the Enterprise.

This view is used to constrain the logical architecture without forcing a particular solution. The L8 view is used for rules which are not expressed as behavioral models, interactions or measures of effectiveness – i.e. they are textual statements of requirement that constrain the architecture.

The L8 view may be used to:

- Define doctrinally correct operational procedures.
- Define business rules.
- Identify operational constraints.

**Implementation**

The L8 view can be represented using:

- An L8 table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L8.PNG']
- An L8 spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L8 table' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Operational Constraint
- Node
- Operational Activity
- Entity Item
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The L8 view is used for rules which are not expressed as behavioral models, interactions or measures of effectiveness – i.e. they are textual statements of requirement that constrain the architecture.</p><p>The L8 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Define doctrinally correct operational procedures.</li><li>Define business rules.</li><li>Identify operational constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L8 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="717c4ec6-4d7e-4b2a-b740-fa6f77c40d40">An L8 table.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image ac:alt="L8 Logical Constraints table" ac:height="331" ac:title="L8 Logical Constraints table" ac:width="627"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L8.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="L8 Logical Constraints" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="717c4ec6-4d7e-4b2a-b740-fa6f77c40d40"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c8891382-42df-48f5-9219-93a881eb0a3d">An L8 spreadsheet report.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating L8 table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Constraint</li><li>Node</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Entity Item</li><li>Operational Exchange</li><li>Exchange Element</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A general specification of the surroundings / scenario in which an operation may take place. Examples would be: “desert”, “arctic”, “at sea”, etc.

DoDAF: A point or extent in space that may be referred to physically or logically. Includes concepts such as: Facility, Installation, RealProperty, Site,, and instances of conditions such as underwater (as specified in UJTLs).

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462397 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00290: Logical Data Model

- page_id: `170462397`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462397/Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T13:08:54.512+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1096998336

1096998339

1096998338

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A LogicalDataModel is a specification of business information requirements as a formal data structure, where relationships and classes (entities) are used to specify the logic which underpins the information.

DoDAF: A Logical Data Model allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Package

##### Related DoDAF views

[DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/DIV-1+Conceptual+Data+Model)

[DIV-2 Logical Data Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/DIV-2+Logical+Data+Model)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-7 Information Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+OV-7+Information+Model)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-7 Information Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024xR1/NOV-7+Information+Model)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462153 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00291: Logical viewpoint

- page_id: `170462153`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462153/Logical+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T10:10:58.144+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['logical-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Logical viewpoint is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. It contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. The Logical viewpoint defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.

#### PANEL: Views

Views

1

It contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170462185 space=UPDM2P2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00292: Lr Lines of Development

- page_id: `170462185`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024xR1/pages/170462185/Lr+Lines+of+Development
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T11:33:46.939+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['lr-lines-of-development', 'lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Lr Lines of Development view specifies the logical threads (lines of development) for a set of projects and programmes. The status of each of these threads can be shown at the various milestones in the projects or programmes. The Lr view is primarily intended to support the acquisition process across multiple projects or programmes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all the DLODs to achieve a successfully integrated military capability.

Use of the Lr view should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cr Capability Roadmap' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']Capability Roadmapview.

The Lr view may be used for:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependencies and the identification of associated risk.
- Portfolio management.
- Through Life Management Planning (TLMP).

**Implementation**

The L3 view can be represented using:

- Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix . [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_program_to_capability.png']
- Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_program_portfolio.png']

- Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart). [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_programme_to_capability_mapping.png']
- Lr Responsibility Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_matrix.png']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']Building Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram' space='UPDM2P2024xR1']
- [Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)](http://docs.nomagic.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=2064481)

****

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Project
- Activity Part Of Capability
- Activity Part Of Project
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Actual Project
- Actual Project Milestone
- Actual Project Milestone Role
- Increment Milestone
- Milestone Sequence
- Organizational Project Relationship
- Out Of Service Milestone
- Project Milestone
- Project Milestone Role
- Project Ownership
- Project Sequence
- Project Sequence
- Project Status
- Project Theme
- Project Type
- Status Indicators

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All project properties are stored in the Architectural Description element which is also the root element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can view, add, or edit project properties in the Specification window of the Architectural Description.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']
